Meal.meal_suggestion: Great quick lunch option. Pair with fruit or veggie sticks; to boost fiber, add extra veggies or use a higher-fiber wrap, and consider reduced-sodium dressing to lower sodium intake.
Meal.nutrition_note: Estimated per-wrap: 350–450 kcal, 25–35 g protein, 5–8 g fiber, 10–15 g fat; sodium approximately 500–800 mg. Provides lean protein and fiber; dressing and cheese contribute to fat and sodium.

Meal.name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Classic Set
Meal.short_description: A quick sheet-pan dinner featuring pork tenderloin, potatoes, and green beans tossed in olive oil and herbs.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with a simple green salad or steamed greens to boost fiber. Season with herbs like rosemary or thyme. For more fiber, swap half the potatoes for sweet potatoes. Leftovers keep well in the fridge for 3–4 days.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean pork tenderloin provides high-quality protein with minimal fat; potatoes supply complex carbs and potassium; green beans add fiber and micronutrients; olive oil delivers heart-healthy fats. For a standard 1-serving portion (about 4–6 oz pork, 1 cup potatoes, 1 cup green beans, 1 tbsp olive oil) expect roughly 30–35 g protein, 35–45 g carbs, 12–15 g fat (~400–500 kcal). To lower calories, use 1 tsp oil or load more veggies; to increase fiber, add a leafy side.

Meal.name: Baked Cod Plate — Sesame Twist
Meal.short_description: Baked cod with roasted sweet potato and broccoli, finished with lemon for a bright, protein-rich plate.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with quinoa or brown rice for extra fiber. If desired, garnish with sesame seeds or a light sesame oil drizzle to mirror the twist.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean cod provides high-quality protein; sweet potato offers complex carbs and beta-carotene; broccoli adds fiber and micronutrients; lemon contributes vitamin C. Optional sesame seeds/oil can add healthy fats to match the 'Sesame Twist'.

Meal.name: Turkey Meatballs & Pasta — Mild-Spice Set
Meal.short_description: Lean turkey meatballs with whole-wheat pasta in tomato sauce, finished with parmesan for calcium and a mild spice profile.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with steamed greens (e.g., broccoli or spinach) to add color and fiber. A typical portion: pasta with turkey meatballs in marinara, topped with parmesan.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean turkey provides protein; whole-wheat pasta offers fiber and complex carbs; parmesan adds calcium. Tomato marinara adds micronutrients but can be high in sodium—use low-sodium sauce and moderate cheese for a lighter option; pair with vegetables for balance.

Meal.name: Miso Breakfast Set — Garlic-Ginger Bake
Meal.short_description: Garlic-ginger miso bake featuring tofu, egg, green onion, and rice—a savory, protein-rich breakfast.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve warm and pair with steamed greens for extra fiber and micronutrients. To tailor, use brown rice or a low-sodium miso variant; vegan option: omit egg or replace with a plant-based protein.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich breakfast (tofu + egg) with rice as the carb base. Miso provides flavor but is high in sodium, so consider low-sodium miso or reducing amount. Contains soy and egg; gluten content depends on miso type (choose gluten-free miso if needed). For more fiber, swap white rice for brown rice.

Meal.name: Fruit & Nut Plate — Citrus Fresh Style
Meal.short_description: A citrus-inspired Fruit & Nut Plate with yogurt, mixed fruit, and nuts for protein, healthy fats, and fiber.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Enjoy as a quick snack or light breakfast: a bowl of yogurt topped with mixed fruit and a small handful of nuts; finish with a squeeze of citrus juice or zest for a bright, refreshing kick.
Meal.nutrition_note: Yogurt provides protein and calcium; nuts contribute healthy fats and satiety; fruit adds fiber, vitamins, and natural sweetness. For a lighter option, choose plain yogurt and unsalted nuts, and add citrus segments for extra freshness.

Meal.name: Greek Yogurt Bowl — Lemon-Herb Variation
Meal.short_description: A protein-packed yogurt bowl with berries, granola, and chia seeds, lightly flavored with lemon and herbs for a refreshing breakfast or snack.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Assemble in a bowl; sprinkle lemon zest and chopped fresh herbs (e.g., mint or basil) to emphasize the lemon-herb variation. Consider a smaller portion of granola to reduce calories, and add a splash of lemon juice for extra tang if desired.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich Greek yogurt supports satiety and muscle recovery. Berries and chia seeds add fiber and omega-3s; granola provides crunch and carbohydrates. Choose a low-sugar granola to keep sugar in check and use lemon zest/herbs to flavor without added sugar.

Meal.name: Tuna Pasta Salad — Classic Skillet
Meal.short_description: A quick skillet-style pasta salad with tuna, cucumber, cherry tomatoes, and olive oil. Protein-forward, with fresh veggies and healthy fats for a simple, balanced meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Use whole-grain pasta for extra fiber, and if available add more vegetables (e.g., spinach, peppers) and a squeeze of lemon for brightness. Keep olive oil portions moderate to manage calories.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein from tuna and olive oil provide healthy fats and satiety; pasta offers energy. Cucumber and cherry tomatoes add fiber and micronutrients. For more fiber, use whole-grain pasta; if sodium is a concern, rinse tuna or choose tuna packed in water.

Meal.name: Lentil Soup — Sesame Variation
Meal.short_description: Lentil soup with carrots, celery, and onion in broth, served with whole-grain bread; sesame variation optional.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve hot with a slice of toasted whole-grain bread. Optional sesame garnish can be added for extra flavor and nutrients.
Meal.nutrition_note: Plant-based protein and fiber from lentils; vegetables provide vitamins/minerals; whole-grain bread adds fiber and complex carbs. Broth-based soup tends to be low in saturated fat, with sodium depending on the stock used. If sesame variation is included, it adds healthy fats and calcium.

Meal.name: Hummus Snack Plate — Berry Boost Twist
Meal.short_description: A quick, balanced snack plate with protein from hummus and cheese, fiber from whole-wheat pita and fresh veggies (cucumber and cherry tomatoes).
Meal.meal_suggestion: Enjoy as a quick snack plate; to align with the Berry Boost Twist, add a handful of berries on the side.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced protein from hummus and cheese; fiber from whole-wheat pita and veggies; healthy fats from hummus. Berry Boost Twist not represented by the current ingredients; add berries for antioxidants if desired. Typical portions: 1–2 pita wedges, 2–3 tbsp hummus, 1 oz cheese, veggies to taste.

Meal.name: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Citrus Fresh Twist
Meal.short_description: A light, satisfying trio of vegetable soup, whole-grain bread, and a side salad with a citrus-inspired twist.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Balanced light lunch option. For extra protein, add chickpeas, lentils, or a dollop of yogurt. To enhance the Citrus Fresh Twist, consider a citrus vinaigrette on the salad.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich from the vegetables and whole-grain bread; provides complex carbs, and vitamins/minerals with low saturated fat. Protein is modest; sodium depends on the soup—opt for a low-sodium option to support heart health. Side salad adds hydration and micronutrients.

Meal.name: Roast Chicken Dinner — Smoky Plate
Meal.short_description: Smoky roast chicken thighs served with quinoa and roasted broccoli and carrots—a protein- and fiber-rich dinner.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Aim for a balanced plate: half vegetables (broccoli and carrots), one-quarter protein (chicken), one-quarter quinoa. For leaner fat, use skinless thighs; boost flavor with smoked paprika or chipotle. Drizzle a small amount of olive oil during roasting for healthy fats.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich meal from chicken thighs with quinoa provides complete protein and fiber. Broccoli and carrots add vitamins A, C, and K along with potassium. Moderate fat from thighs; skinless options reduce fat. Gluten-free and naturally balanced for a dinner.

Meal.name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Berry Boost Skillet
Meal.short_description: One-pan sheet-pan pork tenderloin with potatoes and green beans, seasoned with olive oil and herbs.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Roast on a sheet pan: toss pork tenderloin (whole or sliced into medallions), potato chunks, and trimmed green beans with olive oil, salt, pepper, and herbs (rosemary or thyme). Arrange on a single pan and roast at 425°F (220°C) for 25–30 minutes, until the pork reaches 145–150°F (63–66°C) and potatoes are tender. Rest briefly, then slice pork and serve with the vegetables.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean pork tenderloin offers high-quality protein; potatoes provide energy-rich complex carbohydrates; green beans contribute fiber and micronutrients; olive oil supplies heart-healthy fats. This one-pan approach supports balanced portions and easier cleanup; adjust portions to fit dietary needs.

Meal.name: Ricotta Toast — Garden Veg Variation
Meal.short_description: Fiber-rich ricotta toast topped with berries and a touch of honey for a balanced breakfast or snack.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Toast whole-grain bread, spread with ricotta, drizzle with a small amount of honey, and top with fresh berries. Garden Veg Variation: add thin cucumber slices, tomato wedges, or a handful of greens under the ricotta for extra vegetables.
Meal.nutrition_note: Whole-grain bread provides fiber and sustained energy; ricotta adds protein and calcium; berries deliver vitamins and antioxidants; honey contributes natural sweetness. Suitable as a balanced breakfast or snack; for lower sugar, reduce honey portion.

Meal.name: Smoothie — Smoky Variation
Meal.short_description: Creamy banana peanut butter oat smoothie; a protein- and fiber-rich option with potential smoky flavor tweaks.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serving idea: enjoy as a balanced breakfast or post-workout snack. To adjust macros, use dairy-free milk or lower-fat milk; to boost protein, add Greek yogurt or a protein powder; to introduce a smoky note, blend in a pinch of smoked paprika or a dash of cinnamon.
Meal.nutrition_note: Creamy smoothie built from banana, milk, peanut butter and oats. Rich in protein from peanut butter and milk, with fiber from banana and oats, and healthy fats from peanut butter. Calorie content varies by milk type and portion, but a typical 12–16 oz serving is about 400–480 kcal with roughly 14–22 g protein, 40–60 g carbohydrates and 12–20 g fat.

Meal.name: Greek Yogurt Bowl — Garden Veg Style
Meal.short_description: A creamy Greek yogurt bowl with berries, granola, and chia seeds offering protein, fiber, and healthy fats; easily adaptable to a garden-vegetable theme with a simple veggie garnish.
Meal.meal_suggestion: To emphasize the Garden Veg Style, add a small side of chopped cucumber or tomato or top with fresh herbs. For a veggie boost, stir in a handful of spinach or arugula before serving. Opt for lower-sugar granola if desired.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich Greek yogurt provides calcium and probiotics; berries add fiber and antioxidants; chia seeds contribute omega-3 fats and extra fiber; granola adds complex carbohydrates and crunch. If watching sugar, choose a lower-sugar granola.

Meal.name: Veggie Quesadilla — Smoky Combo
Meal.short_description: Veggie quesadilla with a whole-wheat tortilla, cheddar, bell pepper, spinach, and salsa for a smoky, veggie-forward meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: To lighten it, use reduced-fat cheddar or part-skim cheese; serve with a side of fruit or a simple bean salad for extra fiber and protein.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber from the whole-wheat tortilla and vegetables; calcium and protein from cheddar; moderate fat from cheese; sodium may be elevated from cheddar and salsa.

Meal.name: Veggie Quesadilla — Sesame Wrap
Meal.short_description: A warm veggie quesadilla on a whole-wheat tortilla filled with cheddar, bell pepper, spinach, and salsa.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Pair with a side of fruit or a light yogurt. For more protein in a vegetarian option, add black beans or use bean-based alternative; consider reduced-fat cheddar to lower fat. Optional additions: avocado for healthy fats or a green salad to increase volume and micronutrients.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and vegetables; calcium from cheddar; iron from spinach; vitamins A and C from bell pepper. Moderate sodium from cheese and salsa. To reduce fat and calories, use reduced-fat cheddar and a smaller amount of salsa.

Meal.name: Salmon Salad Wrap — Classic Wrap
Meal.short_description: A classic Salmon Salad Wrap made with canned salmon, Greek yogurt, celery, greens, and a whole-wheat tortilla.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Great for a portable lunch. Use one whole-wheat tortilla and fill with the salmon-yogurt mixture and greens; pair with fruit or veggie sticks for balance. For make-ahead prep, store the filling separately and assemble the wrap just before eating to prevent sogginess.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-packed and rich in omega-3s from salmon and Greek yogurt. Includes fiber from greens, celery, and whole-wheat tortilla. Be mindful of sodium from canned salmon; opt for low-sodium salmon and yogurt if you monitor salt intake.

Meal.name: Shrimp Fried Rice — Lemon-Herb Combo
Meal.short_description: Stir-fried shrimp, egg, peas, carrots, and rice with soy sauce—protein-packed, veggie-rich, and easily customizable with whole-grain rice or lower-sodium seasoning.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Balanced quick meal. To reduce sodium, use low-sodium soy sauce; for more fiber, use brown rice; add a squeeze of lemon and fresh herbs to emphasize the lemon-herb note.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich from shrimp and egg; includes veggies (peas, carrots) and carbs from rice. Soy sauce adds sodium, so consider low-sodium soy sauce or tamari. For more fiber, swap white rice for brown rice; gluten-free option with a gluten-free soy sauce. Lemon zest/herbs can boost flavor without many calories.

Meal.name: Homemade Pizza Night — Garden Veg Combo
Meal.short_description: Homemade garden-veggie pizza with whole-wheat crust, tomato sauce, mozzarella, mushrooms, and bell pepper—colorful and fiber-rich.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve a personal 8–12 inch pizza with a side salad. For a lighter version, use part-skim mozzarella and low-sodium tomato sauce, and consider adding more vegetables or a lean protein if desired.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced: whole-wheat crust (fiber) + mozzarella (protein/calcium) + veggies (vitamins/minerals). Carbs mainly from crust; monitor sodium from cheese/sauce; consider portion control and optional low-sodium ingredients or extra veggies for fiber.

Meal.name: DIY Taco Night — Mild-Spice Style
Meal.short_description: A customizable DIY taco night featuring lean beef, corn tortillas, fresh veggies, cheddar, and salsa with mild spice.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Cook lean ground beef with mild taco seasoning; warm corn tortillas; assemble with lettuce, tomato, cheddar, and salsa. For extra fiber and fats, add beans or avocado. Typical portion: 1–2 tortillas, ~4 oz beef, generous veggies, 1/4 cup cheese.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean beef provides high-quality protein; corn tortillas supply carbohydrates and some fiber; lettuce and tomato add vitamins and additional fiber; cheddar contributes calcium and fat. Monitor sodium from salsa and seasoning—use low-sodium salsa and moderate cheese, and consider adding beans or avocado to boost fiber and heart-healthy fats.

Meal.name: Overnight Oats — Berry Boost Variation
Meal.short_description: Creamy overnight oats with apple and cinnamon—a protein- and fiber-rich breakfast.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Mix ingredients to make overnight oats: rolled oats, milk, plain yogurt, diced apple, and cinnamon. Refrigerate overnight and enjoy cold or warmed.
Meal.nutrition_note: A balanced, protein- and fiber-rich breakfast. Oats provide soluble fiber; yogurt adds protein and calcium; apple contributes vitamins and extra fiber. Cinnamon adds flavor with minimal calories and may help with blood sugar regulation.

Meal.name: Smoothie — Lemon-Herb Style
Meal.short_description: Creamy banana-milk smoothie with peanut butter and oats; balanced protein, carbs, and fats with fiber.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Creamy, protein-forward smoothie great for breakfast or post-workout. For a lemon-herb twist, blend in lemon zest and a few fresh herbs (mint or basil) with the listed ingredients.
Meal.nutrition_note: Approx. 550–600 kcal per serving. Protein ~22–28 g; carbs ~60–70 g; fat ~22–28 g. Rich in potassium (banana), calcium/vitamin D (milk), and fiber (oats). Note: the lemon-herb flavor is not represented by the current ingredients; to add lemon/herb notes, blend in lemon zest and fresh herbs (e.g., mint or basil) — this will adjust macros slightly.

Meal.name: Granola & Milk — Garlic-Ginger Plate
Meal.short_description: A quick bowl combining granola, milk, and banana for a simple breakfast or snack.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Keep as 1 serving granola + 1 cup milk + 1 medium banana. For more protein, use higher-protein milk or add yogurt; to reduce sugar, choose unsweetened granola. If you want a savory nod to the name, add a tiny pinch of minced garlic and grated ginger, but use sparingly as this will alter flavor and nutrition.
Meal.nutrition_note: Carbs from granola and banana; protein and calcium from milk. May be high in added sugars if granola is sweetened; consider unsweetened granola or smaller portions and add fiber with berries or seeds.

Meal.name: Caprese Sandwich — Maple-Kissed Variation
Meal.short_description: Caprese-inspired sandwich on whole-grain bread with mozzarella, tomato, basil, and balsamic; maple-kissed variation optional with a light maple glaze.
Meal.meal_suggestion: A quick, balanced lunch: Caprese sandwich on whole-grain bread with mozzarella, tomato, basil, and balsamic. For the maple-kissed twist, drizzle a small amount of maple-balsamic glaze.
Meal.nutrition_note: Includes fiber from whole-grain bread and protein plus calcium from mozzarella; tomatoes and basil supply vitamins and antioxidants; balsamic adds flavor with modest sugars. A maple-kissed variation can use a light maple-balsamic glaze to introduce sweetness without heavy sugar load.

Meal.name: Lentil Soup — Citrus Fresh Set
Meal.short_description: A hearty, plant-based lentil soup with vegetables served alongside whole-grain bread—protein- and fiber-rich for a vegetarian lunch or dinner.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve hot with warm whole-grain bread; brighten with a squeeze of lemon or lemon zest to echo the Citrus Fresh theme.
Meal.nutrition_note: Plant-based protein and fiber from lentils, plus vitamins/minerals from carrots, celery, and onion. Whole-grain bread adds extra fiber and complex carbs. For lower sodium, use a low-sodium broth; a squeeze of lemon can boost citrus brightness.

Meal.name: Chicken Noodle Soup — Citrus Fresh Twist
Meal.short_description: A comforting chicken noodle soup with carrots and celery in broth, offering protein, vegetables, and warmth with a suggested citrus brightness.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Finish with lemon zest or juice to emphasize the citrus twist; for more fiber, use whole-grain noodles and pair with a small fruit serving for extra vitamin C.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein from chicken and carbohydrates from noodles provide balanced nutrition; carrots and celery add fiber and micronutrients; using a light broth helps keep fat moderate. The citrus twist is not shown in the listed ingredients, so lemon would boost vitamin C if added.

Meal.name: Creamy Tomato Soup with Grilled Cheese Dippers
Meal.short_description: A kid-friendly classic — velvety tomato soup served with crispy whole-wheat grilled cheese strips for dunking.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil, add canned crushed tomatoes and a splash of milk or cream, then blend until smooth. Season with basil, salt, and pepper. Serve in a bowl alongside whole-wheat grilled cheese cut into sticks for easy dipping. A drizzle of olive oil on top adds richness.
Meal.nutrition_note: Tomatoes are rich in lycopene and vitamin C; whole-wheat bread provides fiber and complex carbs; cheddar adds calcium and protein. Milk contributes additional calcium. Keep cream or milk portions moderate to manage fat; using part-skim cheese or reduced-fat milk can lighten the dish while preserving flavor.

Meal.name: Teriyaki Chicken Rice Bowl
Meal.short_description: Sweet and savory teriyaki-glazed chicken over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and shredded carrots — colorful, balanced, and easy to eat.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Pan-sear bite-sized chicken pieces until golden, then toss in a homemade teriyaki glaze (soy sauce, honey, garlic, ginger). Serve over steamed rice with steamed broccoli florets and shredded carrots on the side. Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced scallions for a restaurant-style touch.
Meal.nutrition_note: Chicken breast provides lean protein; broccoli is rich in vitamin C, vitamin K, and fiber; carrots add beta-carotene. Rice supplies energy from carbohydrates. Homemade teriyaki lets you control sugar and sodium — use low-sodium soy sauce and moderate honey to keep it balanced.

Meal.name: Mini Meatball Pasta Bake
Meal.short_description: Tender turkey meatballs baked with whole-wheat penne in marinara sauce and topped with melted mozzarella — hearty comfort food with a nutritious twist.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Mix ground turkey with breadcrumbs, egg, garlic, and Italian seasoning; roll into small meatballs and bake at 400°F for 15 minutes. Toss cooked whole-wheat penne with marinara sauce and the meatballs in a baking dish, top with shredded mozzarella, and bake 10 more minutes until bubbly. Serve with a side of steamed green beans or a small salad.
Meal.nutrition_note: Ground turkey is a leaner alternative to beef, providing protein with less saturated fat; whole-wheat pasta adds fiber for sustained energy; marinara sauce supplies lycopene from tomatoes. Mozzarella provides calcium. Watch portion sizes of cheese and pasta to manage calories; pair with vegetables for a more complete meal.

Meal.name: Whole-Grain Pancakes — Smoky Plate
Meal.short_description: Whole-wheat pancakes with egg and milk, topped with berries and maple syrup.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Pair with a small serving of Greek yogurt or a handful of nuts to boost protein and healthy fats; use a modest amount of maple syrup to keep added sugars in check.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich from whole-wheat flour; good protein from egg and milk; berries provide vitamins and antioxidants. Maple syrup adds sugars, so portion control is helpful. Contains dairy and gluten.

Meal.name: Avocado Toast — Garlic-Ginger Combo
Meal.short_description: Avocado toast on whole-grain bread topped with egg and a lemon finish.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Toast the bread, mash avocado and spread on top, add a cooked egg, and finish with a squeeze of lemon and a dash of pepper. Optional flavor tweaks include adding garlic, ginger, or chili flakes if you like.
Meal.nutrition_note: Well-balanced morning option with fiber from whole-grain bread, heart-healthy fats from avocado, and protein from egg. Lemon adds vitamin C; this combo provides steady energy and satiety. Note: garlic/ginger are not included in the listed ingredients.

Meal.name: Greek Yogurt Bowl — Maple-Kissed Combo
Meal.short_description: A protein-rich yogurt bowl with berries, granola, and chia seeds for a balanced mix of protein, fiber, and healthy fats.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serving idea: 1 cup Greek yogurt, 1/2 cup berries, 1/4 cup granola, 1 tablespoon chia seeds. For added sweetness, drizzle maple lightly; for a lower-sugar version, omit maple and use unsweetened yogurt with a low-sugar granola.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-forward base from Greek yogurt; fiber from berries and chia; calcium and probiotics from yogurt; granola adds crunch and additional carbs and fats. Optional maple drizzle increases sugar; to keep sugar in check, choose unsweetened yogurt and a low-sugar granola.

Meal.name: Bean Burrito Bowl — Berry Boost Style
Meal.short_description: Bean Burrito Bowl with black beans, rice, corn, lettuce, salsa, and cheddar. Protein- and fiber-rich with calcium from cheese; Berry Boost-style variation suggested via addition of fresh berries for antioxidant boost.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Enjoy as-is, then add a Berry Boost by topping with fresh berries or serving a berry salsa alongside. For extra healthy fats, include sliced avocado and a squeeze of lime for brightness.
Meal.nutrition_note: Highlights: good plant-based protein from black beans; fiber from beans, rice, and corn; calcium from cheddar; fat content depends on cheese amount; sodium varies with salsa and cheese. Berry Boost style: add fresh berries (e.g., strawberries, blueberries) on top or mix into salsa to boost antioxidants with minimal extra calories. For a lighter option, consider part-skim cheddar or reducing cheese and adding avocado for healthy fats.

Meal.name: Tuna Pasta Salad — Smoky Wrap
Meal.short_description: Protein-rich tuna pasta salad with cucumber and cherry tomatoes tossed in olive oil; versatile for wraps or quick bowls.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve as a smoky wrap by spooning the tuna pasta salad into a whole-grain tortilla with a handful of greens, or enjoy as a ready-to-eat pasta salad in a bowl.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein from tuna, complex carbohydrates from pasta, and healthy fats from olive oil provide a balanced base. Cucumber and cherry tomatoes add fiber and micronutrients. Canned tuna can be high in sodium, so consider tuna in water and drain well or control portion size to manage sodium and calories.

Meal.name: Turkey Sandwich — Garden Veg Combo
Meal.short_description: A lean turkey sandwich on whole-grain bread with lettuce, tomato, and mustard—protein-rich, fiber-forward, veggie-packed.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Great grab-and-go lunch; pair with a fruit (apple/banana) and a glass of water for a balanced meal.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean protein from turkey + fiber from whole-grain bread; adds veggies (lettuce, tomato) for vitamins and fullness; mustard adds flavor with minimal fat; monitor sodium if using processed turkey or condiments.

Meal.name: Roast Chicken Dinner — Sesame Bake
Meal.short_description: Roast chicken thighs with quinoa, broccoli, and carrots in a sesame style bake for a protein-rich, veggie-forward dinner.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Great for a satisfying, make ahead dinner. For extra flavor, consider topping with sesame seeds before roasting. Serve with a simple side salad to boost greens.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-dense meal from chicken thighs and quinoa; includes fiber from quinoa and vegetables. Provides vitamins A and C from broccoli and carrots, plus minerals like iron and potassium. Moderate fat from chicken thighs; balanced carbohydrates from quinoa and vegetables; low in added sugars.

Meal.name: Pasta Primavera — Classic Style
Meal.short_description: Classic Pasta Primavera featuring pasta, zucchini, mushrooms, tomato, olive oil, and parmesan—a light, veggie-forward Italian-inspired dish.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Add a lean protein such as grilled chicken, shrimp, or chickpeas to increase protein; use whole-grain pasta for extra fiber; serve with a side salad to boost vegetables.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced carbohydrate-forward dish with vegetables for fiber and micronutrients, healthy fats from olive oil, and calcium from parmesan. To boost protein, add grilled chicken, shrimp, or legumes. Consider using whole-grain pasta for more fiber; monitor sodium from cheese.

Meal.name: Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Smoky Style
Meal.short_description: Smoky teriyaki chicken with vegetables and rice
Meal.meal_suggestion: Grill the chicken with a smoky spice rub (paprika or chipotle), serve with 1 cup cooked brown rice and 1.5–2 cups mixed vegetables; use low-sodium teriyaki or homemade to limit sodium المحتوى.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich chicken with carbs from rice and fiber from vegetables. Teriyaki sauce adds sugars and sodium, so consider a low-sodium or homemade sauce to reduce salt; portion control helps manage calories.

Meal.name: Egg Scramble — Berry Boost Combo
Meal.short_description: Protein-packed egg scramble with greens and tomatoes in olive oil, complemented by a berry-side for extra antioxidants.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Egg scramble with spinach and tomatoes cooked in olive oil; pair with a side of berries for a Berry Boost.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich eggs with spinach and tomatoes provide iron, fiber, and vitamin C; olive oil adds heart-healthy fats. This dish is balanced and satiating; adding a side of berries enhances antioxidants to align with the Berry Boost concept.

Meal.name: Avocado Toast — Mild-Spice Combo
Meal.short_description: Toasted whole-grain bread topped with avocado, lemon, and a cooked egg; a quick, balanced meal with an optional mild chili kick.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Assemble: toast whole-grain bread, mash avocado with lemon juice, spread on toast, top with a cooked egg (poached or fried). Season lightly with salt and pepper. For a mild kick, sprinkle a pinch of chili flakes. Serve with sliced veggies if desired.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced choice featuring complex carbs, fiber, healthy fats, and protein. Per serving: roughly 480–520 kcal; protein 15–20 g; carbs 40–50 g; fat 22–28 g; fiber 7–9 g. Lemon adds vitamin C; avocado provides potassium and monounsaturated fats; egg boosts protein. Optional chili flakes offer a mild spice kick without adding significant calories.

Meal.name: Veggie Omelet — Sesame Twist
Meal.short_description: Egg-based veggie omelet with bell pepper, onion, and cheddar, optionally finished with a sesame twist.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with a slice of whole-grain toast or a side of fruit to add fiber. For the sesame twist, sprinkle a teaspoon of sesame seeds on top; you can also swap in a lighter cheese if desired.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich omelet from eggs and cheddar supports satiety and muscle repair. Vegetables provide fiber, vitamin C, and antioxidants; cheddar adds calcium and fat. If using a sesame twist, sesame seeds contribute healthy fats and minerals.

Meal.name: Salmon Salad Wrap — Citrus Fresh Style
Meal.short_description: Protein-packed salmon yogurt salad wrapped in a whole-wheat tortilla with celery and greens.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Mix salmon with Greek yogurt and diced celery; fill a whole-wheat tortilla with greens to assemble a crunchy, protein-forward wrap. Add a splash of lemon juice for the citrus lift if desired.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich wrap with omega-3s from canned salmon and calcium from Greek yogurt. Includes fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and greens plus crunchy celery. Be mindful of sodium in canned salmon; citrus brightness can be added with a squeeze of lemon for the Citrus Fresh Style.

Meal.name: Tuna Pasta Salad — Citrus Fresh Bake
Meal.short_description: A bright, protein-forward pasta salad with tuna, cucumber, and tomatoes dressed in olive oil—easy to prepare and versatile.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Cook pasta until al dente, drain. In a bowl, fold in drained canned tuna, diced cucumber, and halved cherry tomatoes. Drizzle with olive oil and toss to coat. Chill for a refreshing cold salad or serve warm. Optional: add a squeeze of citrus juice or zest to echo the Citrus theme.
Meal.nutrition_note: Excellent protein source from tuna and a balanced portion of carbohydrates from pasta. Olive oil provides heart-healthy fats; vegetables add fiber and micronutrients. If using canned tuna, choose low-sodium or rinse to reduce sodium.

Meal.name: Mediterranean Grain Bowl — Maple-Kissed Wrap
Meal.short_description: Mediterranean grain bowl with quinoa, chickpeas, cucumber, tomato, feta, and olive oil; protein- and fiber-rich with a calcium boost.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve as a bowl or wrap; brighten with lemon juice and fresh herbs (parsley, mint). For a dairy-free version, substitute feta with avocado or olives and reduce olive oil to lower calories.
Meal.nutrition_note: High in plant-based protein and fiber from quinoa and chickpeas; includes calcium from feta and heart-healthy fats from olive oil; moderate sodium due to cheese.

Meal.name: Stuffed Bell Peppers — Garlic-Ginger Wrap
Meal.short_description: Stuffed bell peppers filled with lean turkey, rice, and tomato sauce, topped with cheddar and baked until peppers are tender.
Meal.meal_suggestion: For a lighter version, use brown rice and a smaller amount of cheese; pair with steamed greens or a side salad to boost fiber and micronutrients. You can batch-cook and refrigerate for quick meals.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich stuffed peppers feature lean ground turkey, carbohydrates from rice, and calcium from cheddar. Peppers add fiber and vitamin C; using low-sodium tomato sauce and modest cheese helps manage sodium and saturated fat.

Meal.name: Baked Salmon Plate — Mild-Spice Skillet
Meal.short_description: Baked salmon with mild spices, lemon, mixed vegetables, and rice.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serving idea: 4–6 oz salmon, 1 cup cooked rice, 1–2 cups mixed vegetables; drizzle with lemon juice and a touch of olive oil or herbs. For more fiber, swap white rice for brown rice or quinoa.
Meal.nutrition_note: High-protein, omega-3-rich salmon; moderate carbs from rice; fiber and micronutrients from mixed vegetables; lemon adds vitamin C. Overall balanced and heart-healthy; suitable for portion-controlled dinners. Use minimal added salt if using spice blends.

Meal.name: Chicken Stir-Fry — Berry Boost Style
Meal.short_description: Chicken stir-fry with mixed vegetables and brown rice in a light garlic-soy glaze. Berry Boost styling can be achieved by incorporating berries as a fresh or saucy accompaniment.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with a side of berries (e.g., blueberries or strawberries) or drizzle a berry sauce to honor the Berry Boost concept, while keeping the core protein, veggies, and brown rice for balanced nutrition.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean chicken provides high-quality protein. Brown rice and mixed vegetables supply fiber and essential nutrients; garlic adds flavor and potential immune-supporting properties. Soy sauce contributes sodium, so consider a low-sodium version to keep salt in check. The Berry Boost concept isn’t reflected by the listed ingredients—adding fresh or frozen berries on the side or in a berry-based sauce can boost antioxidants.

Meal.name: Granola & Milk — Garden Veg Bowl
Meal.short_description: A quick granola, milk, and banana bowl offering energy, calcium, potassium, and fiber; customizable with higher-protein milk or added vegetables/fruits.
Meal.meal_suggestion: To boost protein, use higher-protein milk or add Greek yogurt. For more vegetables, top with a handful of spinach or berries to enhance fiber and micronutrients.
Meal.nutrition_note: Carb-rich bowl with protein and calcium from milk; banana adds potassium and fiber. Monitor added sugars in granola; choose unsweetened or lower-sugar varieties and watch portion size.

Meal.name: Avocado Toast — Smoky Skillet
Meal.short_description: Whole-grain avocado toast with lemon and egg, smoky skillet-inspired.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Toast the bread; mash avocado with a squeeze of lemon; spread on toast; top with a fried or poached egg. Optional: a pinch of smoked paprika to add smoky flavor.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced mix: complex carbs and fiber from whole-grain bread; healthy fats from avocado; high-quality protein from egg; lemon adds vitamin C. Helps with satiety and steady energy. Contains gluten and eggs.

Meal.name: Smoothie — Berry Boost Skillet
Meal.short_description: Creamy banana-peanut butter oat smoothie with balanced carbs, protein, and fats.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Blend banana, milk, peanut butter, and oats until smooth for a creamy drink; add ice for a cooler consistency. Optional toppings: sliced banana, berries, or a sprinkle of extra oats.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein- and fiber-rich smoothie from banana, oats, peanut butter, and milk; provides potassium and healthy fats. Use unsweetened milk to limit sugar, and add berries for extra antioxidants if desired.

Meal.name: Caprese Sandwich — Citrus Fresh Variation
Meal.short_description: Caprese-style sandwich on whole-grain bread with mozzarella, tomato, basil, and balsamic—a fresh, protein- and fiber-rich lunch option.
Meal.meal_suggestion: One sandwich serves as a balanced lunch. For the Citrus Fresh Variation, add a light squeeze of lemon or orange juice over the filling or serve with citrus wedges; consider a small side salad for extra greens.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-grain bread and vegetables; protein from mozzarella; vitamins and antioxidants from tomato and basil; balsamic adds flavor with modest calories.

Meal.name: Teriyaki Chicken Bowl — Garden Veg Style
Meal.short_description: Teriyaki chicken bowl with garden vegetables over rice — a protein-rich, veggie-forward meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: A quick, balanced bowl of lean chicken, rice, and mixed vegetables coated in teriyaki glaze. For a lighter version, swap in brown rice and low-sodium sauce to increase fiber and lower sodium.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein from chicken; carbohydrates from rice; fiber and micronutrients from mixed vegetables; teriyaki sauce adds flavor but can be high in sodium; to boost fiber and reduce sodium, use brown rice and/or a reduced-sodium sauce and add more vegetables as desired.

Meal.name: Caprese Sandwich — Maple-Kissed Set
Meal.short_description: Caprese-inspired sandwich on whole-grain bread with mozzarella, tomato, basil, and balsamic.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Enjoy with a side salad or fruit; drizzle a teaspoon of olive oil for healthy fats if desired; keep portion size moderate to fit a balanced meal.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced meal with protein from mozzarella and fiber from whole-grain bread; tomato and basil provide vitamins and antioxidants; balsamic adds flavor with minimal calories; moderate sodium from cheese. For lower fat, use part-skim mozzarella.

Meal.name: Beef & Veggie Stew — Mild-Spice Bake
Meal.short_description: Beef and veggie stew inspired by a mild-spice bake, featuring tender stew beef with potatoes, carrots, and celery simmered in savory broth.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve hot in bowls with a side of steamed greens or a simple salad, and optional crusty whole-grain bread. Garnish with fresh herbs (e.g., parsley) for added flavor without extra calories.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich beef provides essential amino acids; potatoes add energy-rich carbohydrates; carrots and celery supply fiber, vitamins, and minerals; broth contributes moisture and minerals. Sodium varies with broth type; to keep sodium lower, use a low-sodium broth and trim visible fat from the beef.

Meal.name: Shrimp Fried Rice — Citrus Fresh Set
Meal.short_description: Shrimp fried rice with peas, carrots, egg, and soy sauce—a quick, protein-rich grain dish with vegetables.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve as a balanced plate: about 1 cup cooked rice with 4–6 oz shrimp and a generous portion of peas and carrots. To reduce sodium, use low-sodium soy sauce or tamari; substitute white rice with brown rice for more fiber. Pair with a citrus side to emphasize the Citrus Fresh Set.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich from shrimp and egg; carbohydrates from rice; fiber from peas and carrots. Moderate to high sodium from soy sauce; consider low-sodium soy sauce or limiting portion. The Citrus Fresh Set can add vitamin C if paired with a citrus beverage or garnish.

Meal.name: Shrimp Fried Rice — Garlic-Ginger Bowl
Meal.short_description: Garlic-ginger shrimp fried rice bowl with peas, carrots, and egg—protein and starch in a quick, savory dish; mindful of sodium and shellfish allergen.
Meal.meal_suggestion: To boost fiber and reduce sodium: use brown rice or cauliflower rice; choose a low-sodium soy sauce; add more vegetables (peppers, broccoli); reduce oil and use a small amount of sesame oil for flavor.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich due to shrimp and egg; includes carbs from rice and fiber from peas and carrots; may be high in sodium from soy sauce; shellfish allergen present; cooking oil adds fat; can be made lower-sodium and higher-fiber with substitutions.

Meal.name: Apple-Cinnamon Porridge — Mild-Spice Bowl
Meal.short_description: Creamy apple-cinnamon porridge made with oats and milk, lightly spiced for a cozy breakfast.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve warm as a creamy porridge; sprinkle with extra cinnamon and diced apple. Add nuts or yogurt for extra protein if desired.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich oats and apple provide fullness; milk contributes calcium and protein; cinnamon adds flavor with minimal added sugar.

Meal.name: Overnight Oats — Maple-Kissed Style
Meal.short_description: Creamy apple-cinnamon overnight oats with milk and yogurt for a quick, fiber-rich breakfast.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Mix rolled oats, milk, plain yogurt, diced apple, and cinnamon in a jar and refrigerate overnight. In the morning, stir and enjoy cold or warm; add extra cinnamon or chopped apple for texture.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced breakfast with complex carbohydrates from oats, and protein+calcium from yogurt and milk. Fiber from oats and apple; cinnamon adds flavor with minimal calories. If using plain yogurt and unsweetened milk, the total added sugar is low.

Meal.name: Ricotta Toast — Garlic-Ginger Twist
Meal.short_description: Whole-grain toast topped with ricotta, honey, and berries — a protein- and fiber-rich, naturally sweet snack or breakfast.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Toast the whole-grain bread, spread ricotta on top, drizzle with honey, and finish with fresh berries for a simple, balanced meal.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein and calcium from ricotta; fiber and complex carbs from whole-grain bread; natural sugars from honey and berries. This combination makes a balanced breakfast or snack with a moderate amount of fat from dairy. Note: garlic and ginger are not included in the ingredients, so the 'Garlic-Ginger Twist' flavor isn't present in this version.

Meal.name: Mediterranean Grain Bowl — Garlic-Ginger Twist
Meal.short_description: Mediterranean-inspired grain bowl with quinoa, chickpeas, cucumber, tomato, feta, and olive oil, featuring a garlic-ginger flavor twist.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Mix cooked quinoa with chickpeas, cucumber, and tomato. Top with feta and drizzle with olive oil. Optional: finish with a garlic-ginger twist dressing or a squeeze of lemon and fresh herbs.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein- and fiber-rich base from quinoa and chickpeas, plus veggies for hydration. Healthy fats come from feta and olive oil; flavor inspired by a garlic-ginger twist.

Meal.name: Chickpea Salad Pita — Lemon-Herb Set
Meal.short_description: Chickpea salad with cucumber, tomato, and lemon tucked into a whole-wheat pita for a fresh, fiber-rich meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Great as a quick vegetarian lunch. Drizzle with olive oil and herbs for healthy fats, or add feta/t Tahini for extra protein; pair with a side greens for balance.
Meal.nutrition_note: High-fiber, plant-based meal with chickpeas as a protein source, cucumber and tomato for vitamins, and a whole-wheat pita for complex carbs. Lemon adds vitamin C; naturally low in saturated fat.

Meal.name: Chickpea Salad Pita — Citrus Fresh Plate
Meal.short_description: Chickpea salad with cucumber and tomato tucked into a whole-wheat pita, brightened by lemon.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with a side of fruit or a light yogurt. For extra protein, add a spoonful of tahini or a sprinkle of feta; or swap the pita for a gluten-free wrap.
Meal.nutrition_note: High in fiber and plant-based protein from chickpeas; provides complex carbs from whole-wheat pita and vitamins from cucumber, tomato, and lemon (notably vitamin C). Low added fat; sodium varies with canned chickpeas (rinsing helps). Gluten-free option: use gluten-free pita.

Meal.name: Tofu Veggie Curry — Citrus Fresh Wrap
Meal.short_description: Tofu with mixed vegetables in curry sauce served with rice; a protein-rich, veggie-forward plant-based meal with a citrus-inspired fresh note.
Meal.meal_suggestion: To boost fiber, swap white rice for brown rice or quinoa. Opt for a low-sodium curry sauce and add a side of leafy greens for extra volume and micronutrients. A squeeze of lemon or lime can enhance the citrus freshness mentioned in the name.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich tofu provides plant-based protein; fiber from vegetables and rice supports fullness. Carbohydrates mainly come from rice. Depending on the curry sauce, sodium and added sugars may be high; check for dairy and gluten content if you have sensitivities.

Meal.name: Stuffed Bell Peppers — Citrus Fresh Wrap
Meal.short_description: Stuffed bell peppers filled with lean ground turkey, rice, and tomato sauce, topped with cheddar for a savory, protein-rich dish.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with a bright citrus side salad (greens with orange or lemon vinaigrette) to fit the Citrus Fresh Wrap theme, and consider swapping in brown rice and reduced-fat cheese for extra fiber and lower fat.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean ground turkey provides protein; peppers and rice contribute fiber and carbohydrates; cheddar adds calcium and fat; tomato sauce supplies lycopene. Overall a balanced meal with protein, fiber, and modest fat. For a lighter version, use lean turkey, brown rice, and low-sodium sauce.

Meal.name: Baked Cod Plate — Lemon-Herb Skillet
Meal.short_description: Baked cod with lemon-herb flavor, served with roasted sweet potato and broccoli for a light, balanced meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Bake cod and vegetables in a lemon-herb skillet; 25–30 minutes total. Pair with a small portion of quinoa or brown rice for extra whole grains, and a drizzle of olive oil if desired.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean cod provides high-quality protein with low fat; sweet potato and broccoli add fiber, vitamins A, C, and K, and minerals. Lemon adds vitamin C and fresh flavor. Balanced plate with protein, complex carbs, and vegetables.

Meal.name: Miso Breakfast Set — Maple-Kissed Skillet
Meal.short_description: Savory miso skillet with tofu, egg, green onion, and rice—protein-forward breakfast with comforting umami flavor.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Pair with steamed greens or a side salad; drizzle with a tiny amount of sesame oil; for more fiber, use brown rice; to reduce sodium, opt for low-sodium miso or use less miso; add extra egg or tofu to boost protein.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich breakfast from tofu and egg with carbohydrates from rice. Contains soy and sodium from miso; gluten content depends on miso type (use gluten-free miso if needed). For more fiber, swap in brown rice or add vegetables.

Meal.name: Apple-Cinnamon Porridge — Maple-Kissed Bowl
Meal.short_description: Creamy apple-cinnamon porridge made with oats and milk.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve warm as a breakfast bowl. Add a handful of nuts or a spoonful of yogurt for extra protein and healthy fats; optional maple syrup drizzle if desired.
Meal.nutrition_note: High-fiber combination of oats and apple provides steady energy and fullness; milk supplies protein and calcium; cinnamon adds flavor with negligible calories; no added sugars in the listed ingredients.

Meal.name: Overnight Oats — Citrus Fresh Bake
Meal.short_description: Creamy overnight oats with milk, plain yogurt, apple, and cinnamon; a fiber- and protein-forward breakfast with a citrus-inspired twist.
Meal.meal_suggestion: To emphasize the Citrus Fresh concept, add orange zest or a splash of orange juice before refrigerating; pair with a handful of nuts or seeds for healthy fats and extra protein; serve chilled or lightly warmed.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber- and protein-rich breakfast from oats and dairy; apple adds fiber and micronutrients; calcium from milk and plain yogurt supports bone health; cinnamon enhances flavor with little sugar; overall balanced, with sugar level influenced by dairy choice; to lean into the citrus theme, consider adding a touch of orange zest.

Meal.name: Black Bean Wrap — Smoky Bake
Meal.short_description: Smoky black bean wrap with avocado, lettuce, and salsa in a whole-wheat tortilla—fiber-rich, plant-based, and satisfying.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Toast the wrap for extra crispness and heat the filling until beans are warm. To boost protein, add extra black beans or sprinkle hemp seeds; for more smokiness, dust with smoked paprika or chipotle powder. Pair with a side of fruit or raw veggies to round out the meal.
Meal.nutrition_note: Good source of plant-based protein and fiber from black beans, plus healthy fats from avocado. Includes greens and whole grains for satiety; be mindful of sodium in store-bought salsa and wraps—opt for lower-sodium options if needed.

Meal.name: Soba Noodle Bowl — Garlic-Ginger Wrap
Meal.short_description: Light soba noodle bowl with crisp vegetables, protein-rich egg, and a sesame-ginger dressing.
Meal.meal_suggestion: A balanced soba noodle bowl with cucumber, carrot, and egg, dressed in sesame-ginger dressing; can also be enjoyed wrapped in a tortilla for a wrap option.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced: egg provides high-quality protein; soba offers carbohydrates; cucumber and carrot add fiber and micronutrients; sesame dressing contributes healthy fats. If soba is not gluten-free, the dish contains gluten; use 100% buckwheat soba for a gluten-free option. Expect moderate sodium from the dressing.

Meal.name: Black Bean Wrap — Garlic-Ginger Bowl
Meal.short_description: A whole-wheat wrap filled with black beans, avocado, lettuce, and salsa for a plant-based, fiber-rich meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Great as a quick portable lunch. For more protein, add extra beans or a side of yogurt; for a flavor twist, try a light garlic and ginger drizzle or season the beans with garlic powder and ginger during warming.
Meal.nutrition_note: Vegetarian wrap with protein from black beans, fiber from beans and whole-wheat wrap, and healthy fats from avocado. Estimated macros: protein 12–18 g, fiber 9–12 g, fats 9–15 g. Sodium varies with salsa; opt for low-sodium if watching salt.

Meal.name: Homemade Pizza Night — Mild-Spice Wrap
Meal.short_description: Mild pizza-inspired wrap with whole-wheat crust, tomato sauce, mozzarella, mushrooms, and bell pepper — a fiber-rich, veggie-forward dish with calcium and protein.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve as a 1-wrap portion; pair with a large green salad and water to create a balanced meal.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-wheat crust supports fullness; vegetables add vitamins, while mozzarella provides protein and calcium. Monitor sodium and saturated fat from cheese and sauce; for a lighter option, use part-skim mozzarella and a bit less cheese, and pair with a side salad to boost fiber.

Meal.name: Baked Salmon Plate — Maple-Kissed Bake
Meal.short_description: Baked salmon with lemon, served with mixed vegetables and rice.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve salmon with a squeeze of lemon over a bed of rice and accompany with steamed or roasted mixed vegetables for a balanced plate.
Meal.nutrition_note: High-protein meal with omega-3 fats from salmon; complex carbohydrates from rice; fiber and micronutrients from mixed vegetables; vitamin C from lemon. Low in added sugars unless a glaze is used.

Meal.name: Chicken Fajita Bowl — Sesame Wrap
Meal.short_description: A protein-forward fajita bowl with chicken, peppers, onion, rice, and avocado, finished with sesame-inspired flavor.
Meal.meal_suggestion: For more fiber or lower glycemic load, swap white rice for brown rice or cauliflower rice, and keep avocado. If making a wrap, use a sesame wrap with the same fillings and consider adding salsa or lime for brightness.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich chicken with peppers and onion provides fiber and micronutrients; rice supplies energy with carbohydrates; avocado adds heart-healthy fats and satiety. Sesame wrap variation can add extra fats and flavor. Salt content depends on seasoning.

MealPlan_Version.patch_info: Created: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Father > Day 1 > Breakfast/Meal
MealPlan_Version.conversation_history: For fathers greek food filled day, please add a classic american breakfast option.

Meal.name: Greek Protein Power Bowl
Meal.short_description: A high-protein Greek-style breakfast bowl loaded with thick strained yogurt, hard-boiled eggs, feta, chickpeas, and olive oil — over 40g of protein to fuel the morning.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Spoon a generous portion of thick Greek yogurt into a bowl. Halve two hard-boiled eggs and arrange on top with crumbled feta, a handful of roasted chickpeas, sliced cucumber, and cherry tomatoes. Drizzle with extra-virgin olive oil and sprinkle with dried oregano and a pinch of za'atar. Serve with a wedge of whole-wheat pita on the side.
Meal.nutrition_note: Exceptionally high in protein: Greek yogurt (~17g per cup), two eggs (~12g), feta (~6g), and chickpeas (~7g) combine for 40g+ protein. Chickpeas add fiber and iron; olive oil provides heart-healthy monounsaturated fats. Cucumber and tomatoes supply hydration and vitamins C and K. A true Greek-inspired powerhouse breakfast for sustained energy and muscle support.

Meal.name: Shakshuka — Mediterranean Sunrise
Meal.short_description: Eggs poached in a spiced tomato-pepper sauce with cumin and paprika, served with crusty bread for dipping — a vibrant, savory Mediterranean breakfast.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Sauté diced onion, garlic, and bell pepper in olive oil. Add crushed tomatoes, cumin, paprika, and a pinch of chili flakes; simmer 10 minutes. Create wells and crack eggs into the sauce, cover and cook until whites are set. Sprinkle with crumbled feta and fresh parsley. Serve with warm crusty bread or pita for dipping.
Meal.nutrition_note: Eggs provide high-quality protein and choline; tomatoes are rich in lycopene and vitamin C; bell peppers add additional vitamin C and antioxidants. Cumin and paprika offer anti-inflammatory compounds. Olive oil supplies healthy fats. A moderate-calorie, nutrient-dense breakfast; control sodium through feta portions.

Meal.name: Mediterranean Fig & Honey Oatmeal
Meal.short_description: Warm oatmeal topped with dried figs, walnuts, honey, and a swirl of tahini — a Mediterranean twist on a classic breakfast with wholesome flavors.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Cook oats in milk or water until creamy. Top with sliced dried figs, crushed walnuts, a drizzle of raw honey, and a spoonful of tahini. Finish with a light sprinkle of cinnamon and a few pomegranate seeds if available. Pair with a small glass of fresh orange juice for a complete Mediterranean morning.
Meal.nutrition_note: Oats provide soluble fiber (beta-glucan) for heart health and steady blood sugar; walnuts are rich in omega-3 ALA and vitamin E; figs supply potassium, calcium, and iron; tahini adds healthy fats, protein, and calcium. Honey adds natural sweetness — use sparingly. A well-rounded Mediterranean breakfast with complex carbs, healthy fats, and fiber.

Meal.name: Classic American Breakfast Plate
Meal.short_description: A hearty morning plate with scrambled eggs, turkey bacon, whole-grain toast, grilled tomato, and a side of sautéed spinach—comforting, familiar, and balanced.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Scramble two eggs with a splash of milk and a pinch of salt and pepper. Pan-sear two slices of turkey bacon until crisp. Lightly grill or pan-roast a halved tomato until softened. Toast two slices of whole-grain bread and spread a thin layer of butter or avocado. Quickly sauté a handful of spinach in olive oil with a little garlic. Plate the eggs, bacon, toast, tomato, and spinach together and serve with fresh fruit or a small glass of orange juice.
Meal.nutrition_note: Provides a balanced mix of high-quality protein (eggs, turkey bacon), complex carbohydrates and fiber (whole-grain toast, fruit), and vegetables for vitamins and minerals (spinach, tomato). Choosing turkey bacon and minimal added butter keeps saturated fat lower; adding fruit or juice supplies vitamin C and additional fiber when served with whole fruit.

Meal.name: Grilled Chicken Souvlaki Plate
Meal.short_description: Marinated grilled chicken skewers served with fluffy couscous, a crisp cucumber-tomato salad, and creamy tzatziki — a classic Greek lunch plate.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Marinate chicken cubes in olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, and garlic for at least 30 minutes. Thread onto skewers and grill until charred. Serve alongside fluffy couscous, a salad of diced cucumber, tomato, red onion, and kalamata olives, and a generous dollop of tzatziki. Add a lemon wedge and warm pita on the side.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean chicken breast is high in protein and low in saturated fat; couscous provides quick-digesting carbohydrates; cucumber and tomato add hydration, vitamins C and K; olives supply monounsaturated fats; tzatziki (yogurt-based) contributes calcium and probiotics. A balanced Mediterranean plate with protein, carbs, healthy fats, and vegetables.

Meal.name: Mediterranean Lentil & Feta Salad
Meal.short_description: A hearty salad of green lentils tossed with sun-dried tomatoes, feta, arugula, and a bright lemon-herb vinaigrette — plant-powered and filling.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Cook green lentils until tender but not mushy; drain and cool slightly. Toss with chopped sun-dried tomatoes, crumbled feta, baby arugula, diced cucumber, and fresh dill. Dress with a vinaigrette of olive oil, lemon juice, Dijon mustard, and minced garlic. Serve at room temperature; keeps well for meal prep.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lentils are an excellent source of plant-based protein, fiber, iron, and folate; sun-dried tomatoes concentrate lycopene and add umami; feta provides calcium and tangy flavor; arugula offers vitamins A, C, and K. Olive oil and lemon create a heart-healthy dressing. High in fiber for digestive health and blood sugar management.

Meal.name: Spanakopita-Style Stuffed Pita
Meal.short_description: Warm pita pockets filled with a savory spinach-feta mixture inspired by Greek spanakopita — handheld, flavorful, and satisfying.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Sauté fresh spinach with diced onion and garlic in olive oil until wilted. Mix with crumbled feta, a squeeze of lemon, dill, and a pinch of nutmeg. Stuff into warmed whole-wheat pita halves. Serve with a side of Greek salad or hummus and veggie sticks for a complete lunch.
Meal.nutrition_note: Spinach is rich in iron, folate, and vitamins A and K; feta adds protein and calcium; whole-wheat pita provides fiber and complex carbohydrates. Olive oil contributes monounsaturated fats for heart health. A lighter take on traditional spanakopita without the phyllo pastry, reducing saturated fat while keeping all the classic flavors.

Meal.name: Herb-Crusted Baked Sea Bass with Roasted Vegetables
Meal.short_description: Flaky sea bass baked with a Provençal herb crust, served alongside roasted zucchini, eggplant, and cherry tomatoes drizzled with olive oil.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Season sea bass fillets with a mixture of breadcrumbs, dried herbs de Provence, lemon zest, and a drizzle of olive oil. Bake at 400°F for 15–18 minutes. Toss zucchini, eggplant, and cherry tomatoes with olive oil, salt, and pepper; roast on a separate tray. Plate the fish over the vegetables and finish with a squeeze of lemon and fresh basil leaves.
Meal.nutrition_note: Sea bass is a lean white fish high in protein and rich in selenium and B vitamins; roasted Mediterranean vegetables provide fiber, vitamins A and C, and potassium. Olive oil adds anti-inflammatory monounsaturated fats. Herbs de Provence (thyme, rosemary, oregano) contribute antioxidants. A light, elegant dinner aligned with the Mediterranean diet.

Meal.name: Lamb Kofta with Tabbouleh & Hummus
Meal.short_description: Spiced lamb kofta patties served alongside a fresh bulgur tabbouleh salad and creamy homemade hummus — a hearty Mediterranean dinner spread.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Mix ground lamb with minced onion, garlic, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and fresh parsley; form into small oval patties and grill or pan-sear until cooked through. Prepare tabbouleh by tossing fine bulgur with chopped parsley, mint, tomato, and cucumber in a lemon-olive oil dressing. Serve with a generous scoop of hummus and warm flatbread.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lamb provides high-quality protein, iron, zinc, and B12; bulgur wheat is a whole grain rich in fiber and manganese; parsley and mint add vitamins A, C, and K plus fresh flavor. Hummus (chickpeas + tahini) supplies plant protein, fiber, and healthy fats. A satisfying, protein-rich dinner; opt for lean ground lamb to reduce saturated fat.

Meal.name: One-Pot Greek Chicken & Orzo
Meal.short_description: Tender chicken thighs braised with orzo, tomatoes, olives, and artichoke hearts in a lemony broth — a comforting one-pot Mediterranean dinner.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Brown seasoned chicken thighs in olive oil in a deep skillet. Remove and sauté onion and garlic, then add diced tomatoes, chicken broth, orzo, sliced kalamata olives, and canned artichoke hearts. Nestle the chicken back in, cover, and simmer 20 minutes until orzo is tender and liquid is absorbed. Finish with lemon juice, crumbled feta, and chopped fresh oregano.
Meal.nutrition_note: Chicken thighs provide protein with more flavor than breast; orzo supplies carbohydrates; tomatoes and artichoke hearts are rich in fiber, vitamin C, and antioxidants; olives contribute monounsaturated fats. A balanced one-pot meal with protein, carbs, and vegetables. Use low-sodium broth to manage salt; skin-on thighs add richness but also saturated fat — remove skin if preferred.

Meal.name: Apple-Cinnamon Porridge — Mild-Spice Plate
Meal.short_description: Creamy, mildly spiced apple-cinnamon porridge made with oats, milk, and apple.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Dairy-free option: use fortified plant milk. Boost protein by adding a tablespoon of nut butter or a sprinkle of chia/flax seeds. If extra sweetness is desired, drizzle honey or maple syrup. Top with extra sliced apple or a pinch more cinnamon for aroma.
Meal.nutrition_note: Oats supply soluble fiber (beta-glucan) for fullness; milk provides protein and calcium; apple adds vitamins and natural sweetness; cinnamon enhances flavor with minimal added sugar. A balanced, warming breakfast; adjust dairy or sweetness as needed.

Meal.name: Ricotta Toast — Berry Boost Variation
Meal.short_description: Ricotta toast with berries and a light honey drizzle—fiber-rich and protein-boosted for a quick, balanced meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Toast the whole-grain bread, spread ricotta, top with berries, and drizzle honey. Optional: sprinkle a pinch of cinnamon for extra flavor.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein from ricotta; fiber from whole-grain bread and berries; natural sugars from honey and berries. A quick, balanced option—adjust honey to control sugar intake.

Meal.name: Miso Breakfast Set — Classic Set
Meal.short_description: Protein-forward Japanese-inspired breakfast featuring miso, tofu, egg, green onion, and rice.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Prepare a warm miso broth and add tofu cubes and green onion; gently add or scramble an egg and cook to preferred doneness, then serve over steamed rice. Garnish with extra green onion for freshness.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich breakfast with tofu and egg, balanced by rice; provides energy from carbohydrates and healthy fats. Contains soy and is potentially high in sodium due to miso. For lower sodium, choose low-sodium miso and moderate portions.

Meal.name: Bean Burrito Bowl — Smoky Twist
Meal.short_description: Bean Burrito Bowl — Smoky Twist with black beans, rice, corn, lettuce, salsa, and cheddar.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Keep as-is for a balanced vegetarian bowl. For lower fat, use reduced-fat cheddar or reduce cheese; to increase fullness without many calories, add extra salsa or lettuce where possible.
Meal.nutrition_note: High in fiber and plant protein from black beans; includes calcium from cheddar and energy from rice. Corn and lettuce add micronutrients; watch sodium and saturated fat from cheese and salsa; portion control helps with calories.

Meal.name: Caprese Sandwich — Maple-Kissed Bowl
Meal.short_description: Caprese-inspired sandwich on whole-grain bread with mozzarella, tomato, basil, and a balsamic drizzle. Combines complex carbs, calcium-rich dairy, and fresh vegetables for a balanced meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve as a lunch with a side salad or fruit for extra vegetables and fiber. For more protein, add grilled chicken or chickpeas, or use a higher-protein cheese option.
Meal.nutrition_note: Calcium and protein from mozzarella; fiber from whole-grain bread; vitamins from tomato and basil; balsamic adds flavor with minimal calories. Moderate sodium from cheese; balanced mix of complex carbs, protein, and produce.

Meal.name: Chicken Noodle Soup — Garden Veg Bowl
Meal.short_description: A warm garden-vegetable-inspired chicken noodle soup with tender chicken, noodles, carrots, and celery in savory broth.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve hot as a light main dish. To increase fiber and fullness, swap regular noodles for whole-grain noodles and add a handful of leafy greens or chopped spinach. Pair with a piece of fruit for a balanced meal.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced mix of protein (chicken), carbohydrates (noodles), and vegetables (carrots, celery). Provides vitamins A and C from carrots and hydration from broth. For lower sodium, choose low-sodium broth and limit added salt; for extra fiber, use whole-grain noodles or add greens.

Meal.name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Smoky Bake
Meal.short_description: A one-pan sheet-pan dinner featuring smoky-tossed pork tenderloin, potatoes, and green beans roasted with olive oil for a balanced, convenient meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serving idea: 4 oz pork tenderloin, 1 cup potatoes, and 1 cup green beans, roasted with olive oil and smoky spices. Lightly season for flavor and pair with a simple leafy-green salad for extra volume and nutrients.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean protein from pork tenderloin; fiber from potatoes and green beans; healthy fats from olive oil. Provides a balanced mix of protein, complex carbohydrates, and micronutrients; can reduce calories by using a smaller amount of oil and trimming visible fat.

Meal.name: Roast Chicken Dinner — Berry Boost Variation
Meal.short_description: Protein-forward roast dinner featuring chicken, quinoa, and vegetables, with an optional berry-backed antioxidant boost.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Roast chicken thighs with quinoa, steamed broccoli, and carrots. For Berry Boost, add a side of mixed berries or a small berry glaze to introduce antioxidants and a touch of sweetness without overpowering the savory components.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich roast chicken thighs paired with fiber from quinoa and vegetables. Broccoli and carrots provide vitamins C and A, plus minerals. Berry Boost variation can include a berry side or light glaze to add antioxidants; use unsweetened berries to minimize added sugars.

Meal.name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Berry Boost Bake
Meal.short_description: A quick sheet-pan dinner featuring pork tenderloin, potatoes, and green beans roasted with olive oil for a balanced meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Roast pork tenderloin with potatoes and green beans on a sheet pan using olive oil; season lightly and pair with a cup of fresh berries or a berry yogurt for additional flavor and antioxidants.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean pork tenderloin provides high-quality protein; potatoes contribute energy-rich carbohydrates; green beans add fiber and micronutrients; olive oil supplies heart-healthy fats. For a balanced serving, aim for about 3–4 oz pork, 1 cup potatoes, and 1 cup green beans, with olive oil portion kept moderate. Add berries on the side to boost vitamins and antioxidants if desired.

Meal.name: Fruit & Nut Plate — Smoky Combo
Meal.short_description: A balanced snack of mixed fruit, yogurt, and nuts delivering fiber, protein, and healthy fats.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Assemble a serving with 1 cup mixed fruit, ¼–½ cup plain Greek yogurt, and a small handful (about ¼ cup) nuts; enjoy as a portable, balanced snack and adjust portions to fit your daily needs.
Meal.nutrition_note: Provides fiber and antioxidants from fruit, protein and calcium from yogurt, and healthy fats from nuts. To keep sugar and calories in check, choose plain yogurt and unsalted nuts.

Meal.name: Overnight Oats — Citrus Fresh Twist
Meal.short_description: Creamy overnight oats with milk, plain yogurt, apple and cinnamon; a protein- and fiber-rich breakfast that can be brightened with a citrus note.
Meal.meal_suggestion: For a true Citrus Fresh Twist, add citrus zest (orange or lemon) or a small splash of juice to brighten the oats. Top with chopped nuts or seeds for healthy fats and extra crunch. Consider vanilla yogurt or a touch of vanilla extract for depth.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced breakfast with protein and calcium from yogurt and milk, plus fiber from oats and apple. No added sugar in the base ingredients; cinnamon adds flavor and may support blood sugar regulation. Calories vary with dairy choices but it's a filling, nutrient-dense start to the day.

Meal.name: Whole-Grain Pancakes — Citrus Fresh Variation
Meal.short_description: Whole-grain pancakes with egg, milk, berries, and maple syrup—fiber-rich, protein-containing breakfast with natural sweetness.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with a small yogurt or extra berries for added protein or fiber. To echo the citrus theme, garnish with orange zest or add a splash of citrus juice to the batter.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich whole-wheat batter with egg and milk provides sustained energy and high-quality protein; berries add vitamins, fiber, and antioxidants; maple syrup adds sweetness with natural sugars. The Citrus Fresh Variation name suggests optional citrus zest or juice for extra vitamin C and bright flavor, not included in the base ingredients.

Meal.name: Egg Fried Rice — Maple-Kissed Skillet
Meal.short_description: Savory egg fried rice with peas and carrots, seasoned with soy sauce and sesame oil.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with a side of steamed greens or add tofu/chicken for extra protein. Try brown rice for more fiber. Use low-sodium soy sauce and moderate sesame oil to reduce sodium and fat.
Meal.nutrition_note: A balanced quick fried rice dish: carbohydrates from the rice, protein from the egg, and fiber from peas and carrots. Contains healthy fats from sesame oil. Sodium can be high due to soy sauce; consider low-sodium soy sauce or reducing the amount. For more fiber, swap to brown rice; for more protein, add another egg or lean protein on the side.

Meal.name: Hummus Snack Plate — Citrus Fresh Style
Meal.short_description: Colorful Mediterranean-inspired snack plate featuring hummus, whole-wheat pita, veggies, and a cheese component for protein, fiber, and healthy fats.
Meal.meal_suggestion: As a Citrus Fresh-style snack: assemble with 2–3 tablespoons hummus, 1 small whole-wheat pita cut into wedges, cucumber slices, cherry tomatoes, and a small cheese wedge. Add a squeeze of lemon or citrus zest to brighten the flavors. If dairy-free, omit cheese or substitute with a plant-based option.
Meal.nutrition_note: This plate provides protein from hummus and cheese, plus fiber from whole-wheat pita and vegetables, and healthy fats from tahini in hummus. It offers a balanced mix of complex carbs, protein, and fats. Watch sodium from store-bought hummus and cheese; consider reduced-sodium options or smaller portions if needed. For a lighter version, you can reduce cheese or use part-skim options.

Meal.name: Veggie Quesadilla — Classic Bake
Meal.short_description: A baked veggie quesadilla with whole-wheat tortilla, cheddar, bell pepper, spinach, and salsa.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with a side salad or fruit. For a lighter version, use part-skim cheese or reduce cheese amount; add avocado for healthy fats and extra fiber.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and vegetables; protein and calcium from cheddar. Spinach and bell pepper supply vitamins A and C; salsa adds flavor with relatively few calories. If watching sodium, choose low-sodium cheese or salsa.

Meal.name: Turkey Meatballs & Pasta — Berry Boost Combo
Meal.short_description: Turkey meatballs with whole-wheat pasta in marinara, topped with parmesan; Berry Boost indicates a berry-based fruit side for added antioxidants.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with a side of mixed berries or a small berry compote to fit the Berry Boost concept; add steamed greens for extra fiber and micronutrients.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean ground turkey provides high-quality protein; whole-wheat pasta adds fiber and complex carbs; marinara delivers tomatoes and flavor; parmesan contributes calcium. The Berry Boost name suggests pairing with a berry fruit side for extra antioxidants and vitamins.

Meal.name: Roast Chicken Dinner — Berry Boost Twist
Meal.short_description: Roasted chicken dinner with quinoa and vegetables (broccoli and carrots), adaptable with a berry-enhanced twist for extra antioxidants.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Suggested portion: 4–6 oz cooked chicken thighs; 1/2–3/4 cup cooked quinoa; 1 cup broccoli; 1 cup carrots. Optional: include a side of berries or a berry glaze to fit the Berry Boost Twist.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich with chicken thighs and quinoa; fiber and vitamins from broccoli and carrots. Berry Boost Twist can be realized by adding fresh berries or a berry sauce on the side for extra antioxidants.

Meal.name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Garden Veg Style
Meal.short_description: Sheet-pan pork dinner featuring pork tenderloin, potatoes, green beans, and olive oil.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Roast pork tenderloin with potatoes and green beans on a sheet pan, drizzled with olive oil and seasoned with garlic and herbs for a simple, nutritious dinner.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean pork provides high-quality protein; potatoes supply complex carbs and potassium; green beans add fiber and micronutrients; olive oil contributes heart-healthy fats. For a balanced portion, aim for about 4–6 oz pork with 1–2 cups vegetables and a moderate potato serving per person.

Meal.name: Miso Breakfast Set — Sesame Variation
Meal.short_description: Miso breakfast set with tofu, egg, green onion, rice, and optional sesame variation for a protein-packed savory morning meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve warm as a balanced bowl: mix in the tofu with a small amount of miso for flavor, top with green onion and a gently cooked egg, and accompany with steamed rice. If sesame variation is used, finish with toasted sesame seeds for extra texture; opt for low-sodium miso to keep sodium in check.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich breakfast from tofu and egg supports satiety and muscle maintenance. Includes rice for steady energy; miso provides fermentation-associated flavor and potential gut benefits. Sodium intake can be high from miso paste—consider low-sodium miso if needed. Sesame variation may add healthy fats and a nutty flavor if sesame is used.

Meal.name: Breakfast Sandwich — Sesame Bowl
Meal.short_description: A savory breakfast sandwich on a whole-wheat English muffin with egg, cheddar, and tomato.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Quick, balanced breakfast. To adjust: add another egg or more cheese for extra protein; use egg whites and light cheese to lower calories; choose a higher-fiber muffin to boost fiber.
Meal.nutrition_note: Approx 320–420 kcal; protein 15–20 g; carbs 30–40 g; fat 9–12 g. Fiber from the whole-wheat muffin; tomato adds micronutrients. Sodium varies with cheese; consider lower-sodium cheddar to reduce salt.

Meal.name: Breakfast Wrap — Smoky Variation
Meal.short_description: Smoky breakfast wrap with egg, turkey, spinach in a whole-wheat tortilla and salsa.
Meal.meal_suggestion: For lower sodium, use low-sodium turkey and salsa; add avocado for healthy fats to enhance fullness and flavor.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced breakfast wrap with lean protein (turkey and egg) and fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and spinach. Monitor sodium from turkey and salsa; consider low-sodium options if needed.

Meal.name: Turkey Sandwich — Classic Set
Meal.short_description: Classic turkey sandwich on whole-grain bread with lettuce, tomato, and mustard.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Pair with a piece of fruit and a small yogurt for a complete lunch; add extra veggie slices or a side salad to boost fiber.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich turkey and fiber from whole-grain bread provide a balanced base with vitamins from lettuce and tomato. Low in fat; sodium can be moderate—opt for low-sodium turkey and mustard if salt is a concern.

Meal.name: Teriyaki Chicken Bowl — Classic Variation
Meal.short_description: Teriyaki Chicken Bowl — Classic variation with chicken, rice, mixed vegetables, and teriyaki sauce.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Aim for a balanced portion: 3–4 oz cooked chicken, 1 cup cooked brown rice, and 1.5–2 cups mixed vegetables. Use low-sodium teriyaki or dilute the sauce with water, and finish with sesame seeds or sliced green onions for flavor without excess calories.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich chicken supports satiety; rice provides energy; mixed vegetables add fiber, vitamins, and minerals. Teriyaki sauce contributes flavor but can add sodium and added sugars. For a healthier option, swap white rice for brown rice or quinoa, and use a low-sodium or diluted sauce to reduce sodium intake.

Meal.name: Grilled Cheese & Soup — Lemon-Herb Variation
Meal.short_description: Grilled cheese on whole-grain bread with cheddar, served with tomato soup and grapes—a simple, fiber-rich meal with protein, calcium, and fruit.
Meal.meal_suggestion: For a lighter option, use low-sodium tomato soup and/or reduced-fat cheddar; keep the whole-grain bread for fiber. Pair with a side salad or steamed veggies to boost micronutrients and volume without excessive calories.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-grain bread and grapes; calcium and protein from cheddar; tomato soup adds vegetables. Moderate sodium from cheese and soup; consider low-sodium tomato soup and/or reduced-fat cheese to lower sodium and saturated fat. The lemon-herb variation isn't reflected in the listed ingredients; to capture that flavor, add lemon zest and herbs to the sandwich or soup.

Meal.name: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Lemon-Herb Bowl
Meal.short_description: Plant-based meal: vegetable soup, whole-grain bread, and a side salad—light, fiber-rich, and satisfying.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Boost protein by adding beans, lentils, or tofu to the soup or salad; use a low-sodium soup; drizzle the salad with olive oil and lemon for healthy fats.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich and vitamin-packed with vegetables and whole-grain bread; protein is moderate; sodium varies by soup; opt for low-sodium soup for heart health.

Meal.name: Stuffed Bell Peppers — Mild-Spice Style
Meal.short_description: Mild-spice style stuffed bell peppers filled with lean ground turkey, rice, tomato sauce, and cheddar.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve warm as a complete meal. Pair with a side salad or steamed greens to boost fiber; substitute brown rice for more texture and nutrition; adjust cheese amount for a lighter or richer flavor.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean ground turkey provides high-quality protein; rice supplies quick energy carbs; bell peppers add fiber and vitamin C.
