Cheddar adds calcium and flavor, contributing some saturated fat and sodium depending on cheese and sauce. For a lighter version, choose low-sodium tomato sauce and lean turkey.

Meal.name: Tofu Veggie Curry — Citrus Fresh Combo
Meal.short_description: Tofu and mixed vegetables in curry sauce over rice, a quick, plant-based curry meal with citrusy freshness.
Meal.meal_suggestion: For more fiber, swap white rice for brown rice or serve with a side salad; add a squeeze of lemon or lime for extra citrus brightness.
Meal.nutrition_note: Plant-based protein from tofu, fiber from mixed vegetables, and carbohydrates from rice. The curry sauce adds flavor and sodium; consider portion control or choosing a low-sodium option if needed.

Meal.name: Banana Bread Toast — Sesame Combo
Meal.short_description: Banana bread toast topped with yogurt and berries for a protein-fiber balanced snack.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Toast a slice of banana bread; spread with Greek yogurt; top with fresh berries. If desired, sprinkle a small amount of sesame seeds for crunch and extra minerals.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein: moderate from Greek yogurt; carbs from banana bread and berries; fiber from berries (and banana bread depending on recipe). Fat varies with yogurt type; watch added sugars in banana bread. This combo provides calcium and vitamins from dairy and fruit; suitable as a quick breakfast or snack.

Meal.name: Apple-Cinnamon Porridge — Maple-Kissed Variation
Meal.short_description: Creamy apple-cinnamon porridge made with oats and milk, with an optional maple-sweetened variation.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve warm as a comforting breakfast. For extra protein, top with yogurt or nuts; if you want the maple-kissed variation, swirl in a small amount of maple syrup.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich oats and apple support fullness; milk adds protein and calcium; cinnamon adds flavor with minimal added sugar. Maple-kissed variation suggests a light maple sweetness—add maple syrup sparingly if desired.

Meal.name: Whole-Grain Pancakes — Mild-Spice Plate
Meal.short_description: Whole-grain pancakes with berries and maple syrup—fiber-rich, protein-present, and naturally sweet with a touch of mild spice.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Pair with Greek yogurt or additional berries for extra protein and fiber. Serve warm with a light drizzle of maple syrup; optionally add a pinch of cinnamon for a mild spice note.
Meal.nutrition_note: High-fiber, protein-containing plate from whole-wheat flour, egg, and milk; antioxidants from berries; natural sugars from maple syrup. To limit added sugars, drizzle sparingly and consider lighter syrup or fruit topping.

Meal.name: Lentil Soup — Garden Veg Bowl
Meal.short_description: A hearty lentil soup with carrots, celery, and onions in broth, paired with whole-grain bread for a balanced plant-based meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve hot lentil-vegetable soup with a slice of toasted whole-grain bread; consider a squeeze of lemon or fresh herbs for brightness.
Meal.nutrition_note: Plant-based protein and fiber from lentils; vegetables add vitamins and minerals; whole-grain bread boosts filling carbohydrates and fiber. If sodium is a concern, use low-sodium broth and monitor portion size.

Meal.name: Grilled Cheese & Soup — Classic Skillet
Meal.short_description: Classic skillet grilled cheese on whole-grain bread with cheddar, tomato soup, and grapes.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Consider adding a side salad or steamed vegetables to boost veggie intake; use a smaller slice of cheddar or a lower-fat cheese to reduce saturated fat; choose a low-sodium tomato soup to help manage sodium.
Meal.nutrition_note: Provides protein from cheddar and fiber from whole-grain bread, plus antioxidants from grapes. Tomato soup adds volume but can be high in sodium; opt for low-sodium soup and moderate cheese portions for a balanced meal.

Meal.name: Grilled Cheese & Soup — Garlic-Ginger Bake
Meal.short_description: Grilled cheese on whole-grain bread with cheddar, tomato soup, and grapes.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Grilled cheese on whole-grain bread with cheddar, served with tomato soup and a side of grapes. To make it lighter, choose reduced-sodium soup and a smaller cheese portion; add a simple side salad for more vegetables.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced mix: complex carbs from whole-grain bread and grapes, protein and calcium from cheddar, and vegetables from tomato soup. Tomato soup can be high in sodium; opt for low-sodium or homemade if possible. Grapes add fruit and fiber; consider adding extra veggies to boost fiber and micronutrients.

Meal.name: Bean & Cheese Burritos — Garlic-Ginger Bake
Meal.short_description: Warm garlic-ginger baked burritos with black beans, cheddar, salsa, and greens in whole-wheat tortillas.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with extra greens or fresh salsa; add a piece of fruit on the side. For a lighter version, use reduced-fat cheese or low-sodium salsa; to boost protein, add a dollop of Greek yogurt on the side.
Meal.nutrition_note: High in fiber and plant-based protein from black beans and whole-wheat tortillas; calcium from cheddar; greens add vitamins and minerals. The dish can be higher in sodium from cheese and salsa, so consider low-sodium cheese/salsa or add more vegetables to increase volume with less salt.

Meal.name: Homemade Pizza Night — Sesame Twist
Meal.short_description: Wholesome whole-wheat crust pizza with tomato sauce, mozzarella, mushrooms, and bell peppers; sesame twist adds extra flavor and fats if used.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Top with sesame seeds (optional); choose part-skim mozzarella; load with peppers and mushrooms; serve with a simple green salad.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-wheat crust; calcium and protein from mozzarella; lycopene from tomato sauce; vitamins A and C from bell peppers; mushrooms add minerals. Optional sesame seeds provide healthy fats. For lower sodium, use reduced-sodium sauce and lighter cheese; pair with a side salad for more veggies.

Meal.name: Stuffed Bell Peppers — Garlic-Ginger Bake
Meal.short_description: Baked stuffed bell peppers filled with ground turkey, rice, tomato sauce, and cheddar.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve 1–2 stuffed peppers per person with a green salad or steamed vegetables; for lower calories, use brown rice and reduced-fat cheddar; can be made ahead and reheated.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean turkey provides protein; rice gives carbs; peppers add fiber and vitamins; cheddar supplies calcium and fat. Tomato sauce adds lycopene; sodium varies by sauce—use low-sodium options to reduce sodium.

Meal.name: Breakfast Wrap — Citrus Fresh Bake
Meal.short_description: A quick, balanced breakfast wrap with a whole-wheat tortilla, egg, turkey, spinach, and salsa.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Pair with a piece of fruit (e.g., orange) for extra vitamin C, and consider a squeeze of lime or a bit of avocado for freshness. To reduce sodium, use low-sodium turkey and homemade salsa.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich from egg and turkey; fiber and micronutrients from whole-wheat tortilla and spinach; salsa adds flavor and vitamin C. Mind sodium: turkey and salsa can be high-sodium—choose low-sodium turkey or add extra veggies to boost volume.

Meal.name: Breakfast Wrap — Lemon-Herb Skillet
Meal.short_description: A warm breakfast wrap featuring a whole-wheat tortilla, scrambled egg, turkey slices, spinach, and salsa, cooked in a lemon-herb skillet.
Meal.meal_suggestion: A quick, balanced breakfast wrap with protein, fiber, and vegetables; pair with fruit or yogurt for a fuller morning meal.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich meal thanks to egg and turkey; fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and spinach; salsa adds flavor with minimal added sugar. Sodium can be modest-to-high depending on turkey and salsa—opt for low-sodium versions if needed. For more fiber, add extra greens or use a higher-fiber tortilla; for lower fat, use egg whites and a smaller tortilla.

Meal.name: Breakfast Wrap — Smoky Combo
Meal.short_description: Protein-packed breakfast wrap with egg, turkey, spinach, and salsa in a whole-wheat tortilla.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Suggestions: swap turkey for chicken or plant-based protein; add avocado for healthy fats; include additional colorful veggies (bell peppers, onions); use low-sodium salsa or serve as a bowl to vary texture.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich breakfast with egg and turkey, plus fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and spinach. Includes vegetables and salsa; watch sodium levels from turkey and salsa—choose low-sodium options or reduce added salt. Optional avocado adds healthy fats.

Meal.name: Chicken Noodle Soup — Citrus Fresh Combo
Meal.short_description: A comforting chicken noodle soup with carrots and celery in a light broth, finished with a citrus-bright touch.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Tips: switch to low-sodium broth and whole-grain noodles for more fiber; add leafy greens (spinach or kale) for extra nutrients; finish with lemon juice or zest to enhance the citrus note and aroma.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean chicken provides protein; noodles deliver carbohydrates; carrots and celery add fiber and micronutrients; broth contributes hydration and sodium (use low-sodium broth to reduce sodium). To honor the Citrus Fresh theme, finish with a squeeze of lemon juice or lemon zest for vitamin C and brightness without added fat.

Meal.name: Turkey Sandwich — Mild-Spice Combo
Meal.short_description: A mild-spice turkey sandwich on whole-grain bread with lettuce, tomato, and mustard.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Keep as a simple, balanced lunch: turkey sandwich on whole-grain bread with lettuce and tomato. Pair with fruit or carrot sticks for more fiber. To adjust spice, use a milder mustard or add a small amount of hot sauce if acceptable.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean turkey provides protein; whole-grain bread adds fiber; lettuce and tomato supply vitamins. Moderate sodium from deli turkey and mustard; consider low-sodium options if needed. Contains gluten (bread).

Meal.name: Egg Fried Rice — Lemon-Herb Skillet
Meal.short_description: Egg fried rice with peas and carrots, seasoned with soy sauce and sesame oil; a quick protein- and veggie-packed skillet meal (lemon-herb flavor notes optional).
Meal.meal_suggestion: One-pan dish ready in minutes. Serve as is or with a side salad or steamed greens. To adjust nutrition, swap in brown rice for more fiber and/or reduce oil; use low-sodium soy sauce to cut sodium. Optional lemon zest and herbs can brighten the flavor.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich egg with rice provides a balanced mix of carbs and protein; vegetables add fiber and micronutrients (vitamin A from carrots, vitamin C from peas). Sesame oil contributes flavor and healthy fats; soy sauce increases sodium, so consider low-sodium soy sauce or reduce amount. For more fiber, use brown rice or add extra veggies; for brighter flavor, add lemon zest/juice and fresh herbs (optional).

Meal.name: Roast Chicken Dinner — Maple-Kissed Bowl
Meal.short_description: Roast chicken thighs with quinoa, broccoli, and carrots for a protein-forward, fiber-rich veggie bowl.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve warm: roasted chicken thighs with fluffy quinoa and steamed broccoli and carrots; the name suggests a maple-kissed flavor—top with a light maple glaze if you like.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich chicken thighs provide satiety; quinoa offers complete protein and fiber; broccoli and carrots add vitamins A and C plus fiber, making this a balanced bowl with moderate fat from the thighs.

Meal.name: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Garden Veg Bowl
Meal.short_description: A fiber-forward garden veggie meal featuring vegetable soup, whole-grain bread, and a fresh side salad.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Great as a light, balanced lunch or dinner. For extra protein, add beans, lentils, or hummus to the soup or salad; drizzle a little olive oil on the salad for healthy fats; choose low-sodium soup to manage salt intake.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich, plant-based meal with vegetables, whole grains, and a side of greens. Provides complex carbs from whole-grain bread and essential vitamins; protein is modest—consider adding a protein source if you need more satiety. Watch sodium levels in soup; opt for low-sodium options when possible.

Meal.name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Sesame Skillet
Meal.short_description: One-pan pork tenderloin with potatoes and green beans roasted in olive oil.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve as a complete dinner: pair with a side salad or steamed greens and a grain (e.g., quinoa) for extra fiber. Season with herbs and a light seasoning of salt if desired.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean pork tenderloin provides high-quality protein; potatoes supply complex carbohydrates; green beans add fiber and micronutrients; olive oil contributes heart-healthy fats. This one-pan meal can fit into a balanced diet when portions are appropriate.

Meal.name: Oatmeal — Lemon-Herb Set
Meal.short_description: Creamy oatmeal with banana and almonds for a satisfying, wholesome breakfast.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Enjoy warm as a filling breakfast bowl. To echo the Lemon-Herb theme, add a splash of lemon zest and herbs, or top with fresh berries for extra flavor and antioxidants.
Meal.nutrition_note: High in fiber from oats, protein from milk and almonds, and natural sugars from banana. Provides calcium and potassium with healthy fats; a balanced, satiating breakfast.

Meal.name: Fruit & Nut Plate — Berry Boost Bake
Meal.short_description: A quick, balanced plate of mixed fruit, nuts, and yogurt with a berry-boosted flavor profile.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Portion idea: 1 cup mixed fruit, 1/2 cup yogurt, and a small handful (about 1 oz / 28 g) of nuts. Add cinnamon or a light drizzle of honey if desired. For higher protein, use Greek yogurt.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber- and vitamin-rich fruit paired with yogurt provides protein and calcium; nuts add healthy fats and satiety. Natural sugars come from the fruit, so watch portions if you're monitoring calories.

Meal.name: Banana Bread Toast — Classic Bowl
Meal.short_description: Banana bread toast topped with Greek yogurt and berries—a quick, balanced breakfast with protein, carbs, and fiber.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Toast a slice of banana bread, spread with Greek yogurt, and top with a handful of fresh berries.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced mix of carbs, protein, and micronutrients: banana bread provides energy; Greek yogurt supplies protein and calcium; berries deliver fiber and antioxidants. For a lighter option, use whole-grain banana bread and low-fat yogurt.

Meal.name: Veggie Quesadilla — Garlic-Ginger Variation
Meal.short_description: A vegetarian quesadilla on a whole-wheat tortilla with cheddar, bell pepper, spinach, and salsa. Fiber and calcium-rich with veggie nutrients, ready in a quick, balanced meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Pair with a light side salad or fruit to round out the meal; for higher protein, add a lean protein (beans, tofu, or chicken) or include more beans inside the quesadilla; using reduced-fat cheese can lower overall saturated fat.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich from whole-wheat tortilla and vegetables; provides calcium and protein from cheddar; includes vitamins A, C, and K from peppers and spinach; salsa adds vitamin C. Watch sodium from cheese and salsa; consider reduced-fat cheese or low-sodium salsa to lower saturated fat and sodium.

Meal.name: Caprese Sandwich — Mild-Spice Bowl
Meal.short_description: Caprese-inspired sandwich with whole-grain bread, mozzarella, tomato, basil, and balsamic for a mild, fresh flavor.
Meal.meal_suggestion: For a bowl version, serve components over greens or quinoa and drizzle with balsamic. To boost protein, add grilled chicken or chickpeas. Pair with fruit or veggie side to round out the meal and keep portions mindful of sodium.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-grain bread plus protein and calcium from mozzarella; tomatoes and basil add vitamins. Balsamic adds flavor with modest sugar; watch sodium from cheese. Mild, vegetarian option.

Meal.name: Egg Fried Rice — Mild-Spice Bake
Meal.short_description: A baked, mildly spiced version of egg fried rice with rice, egg, peas, carrots, soy sauce and sesame oil.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve warm with a side of leafy greens or cucumber salad to balance sodium; for fiber boost, substitute brown rice and opt for low-sodium soy sauce.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein from egg and carbs from rice; includes fiber and micronutrients from peas and carrots. Sesame oil adds healthy fats. Soy sauce adds sodium—use low-sodium soy sauce or reduce amount. For more fiber, swap white rice with brown rice.

Meal.name: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Lemon-Herb Wrap
Meal.short_description: Fiber-rich, plant-forward meal: vegetable soup, whole-grain bread, and a side salad.
Meal.meal_suggestion: To increase protein and fullness, add a protein source (beans, lentils, yogurt, or grilled poultry). Use a low-sodium soup option and drizzle a small amount of olive oil on the salad for healthy fats. Consider adding a piece of fruit for dessert.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich meal from vegetables and whole grains; provides complex carbs, vitamins, and minerals with low saturated fat. Sodium varies with soup—opt for a low-sodium option to keep sodium reasonable.

Meal.name: DIY Taco Night — Garlic-Ginger Skillet
Meal.short_description: Lean beef tacos on corn tortillas with fresh veggies and cheddar—balanced, customizable DIY taco night.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Assemble 2 warm corn tortillas and fill with 3–4 oz lean ground beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, a tablespoon of salsa, and a light sprinkle of cheddar. Optional: top with avocado for healthy fats.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich lean beef with fiber from lettuce, tomato, salsa and corn tortillas; calcium from cheddar. Monitor sodium from salsa and cheese. For a lighter option, use 2 tortillas, 3–4 oz beef, and low-sodium salsa or reduced-fat cheddar.

Meal.name: DIY Taco Night — Maple-Kissed Skillet
Meal.short_description: A quick, customizable taco night featuring lean beef, corn tortillas, fresh veggies, cheddar, and salsa.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Assemble made‑to‑order tacos: warm corn tortillas, top with lean beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, cheddar, and salsa. Optional lime, avocado, or light sour cream. Pair with a simple side salad or beans for extra fiber.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean beef provides high‑quality protein; corn tortillas supply carbohydrates; lettuce and tomato add volume and micronutrients; cheddar contributes calcium and fat; salsa offers flavor with relatively low calories. To boost fiber, use whole‑grain tortillas or add beans; to reduce sodium, opt for low‑sodium salsa and lighter cheese.

Meal.name: Cottage Cheese Bowl — Citrus Fresh Bowl
Meal.short_description: Protein-packed cottage cheese bowl with pineapple and cinnamon for a tangy, tropical-inspired breakfast or snack.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Cottage cheese bowl topped with pineapple and a light dusting of cinnamon. Enjoy as a quick high-protein breakfast or snack. For more fiber and staying power, add berries, chia seeds, or a small handful of nuts.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich cottage cheese delivers satiety and calcium; pineapple contributes vitamin C and natural sweetness with modest sugars. Cinnamon adds aroma and may modestly aid blood sugar regulation.

Meal.name: Smoothie — Sesame Twist
Meal.short_description: Creamy banana oat smoothie with milk and peanut butter; protein- and fiber-rich, balanced and satisfying.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Great as a quick breakfast or post-workout drink. To honor the Sesame Twist, top with sesame seeds or swirl in a small amount of tahini; optionally add a pinch of cinnamon or vanilla for extra aroma.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced smoothie with carbohydrates from banana and oats, protein from milk and peanut butter, and healthy fats from peanut butter. Good source of calcium and potassium; can be calorie-dense, so adjust portions to fit your needs. For a Sesame Twist, consider topping with sesame seeds or swirling in a bit of tahini.

Meal.name: Egg Scramble — Garlic-Ginger Bake
Meal.short_description: An egg scramble with spinach and tomatoes cooked in olive oil; a simple, protein-forward dish featuring vegetables.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Best as a quick breakfast or light lunch; pair with whole-grain toast or fruit for extra fiber and complex carbs.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich eggs provide essential amino acids and satiety; olive oil contributes heart-healthy fats; spinach and tomatoes add fiber, vitamins A, C, and K, and minerals—resulting in a balanced, quick breakfast.

Meal.name: Bean Burrito Bowl — Garden Veg Bake
Meal.short_description: A baked bean burrito bowl featuring black beans, rice, corn, lettuce, salsa, and cheddar—vegetarian and hearty with garden-veg appeal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Great for a quick, plant-forward meal or meal prep. To balance macros, consider brown rice and/or extra beans, use reduced-fat cheese or plant-based cheese, and add extra veggies or avocado for healthy fats.
Meal.nutrition_note: Bean-based protein plus fiber from beans and corn; contains calcium from cheddar and some saturated fat. Includes vegetables from lettuce and salsa. Overall provides complex carbs and protein, but can be higher in sodium and fat due to cheese and salsa. For a lighter version, use reduced-fat cheddar or omit cheese, swap white rice for brown rice, and add more veggies (e.g., peppers or avocado) for extra nutrients.

Meal.name: Soba Noodle Bowl — Mild-Spice Style
Meal.short_description: Soba noodles with cucumber, carrots, and egg in a mild-spice sesame dressing.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Great as a light lunch. To boost protein, add tofu or grilled chicken; add greens like spinach or bok choy; reduce sodium by using a low-sodium sesame dressing or serving the dressing on the side.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced bowl with complex carbs from soba, fiber from cucumber and carrots, and protein from egg. Contains sesame and egg; gluten content depends on soba (many varieties contain wheat). Sodium can be moderate to high from the dressing. Mild spice; avoid if sesame or egg allergies.

Meal.name: Black Bean Wrap — Classic Combo
Meal.short_description: A wholesome plant-based wrap featuring a whole-wheat tortilla, black beans, avocado, lettuce, and salsa for a satisfying, fiber- and protein-rich meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with a side of fruit or veggie sticks; squeeze lime and sprinkle cilantro for freshness; ideal for meal prep as a make-ahead wrap.
Meal.nutrition_note: Plant-based, fiber-rich meal with protein from black beans and healthy fats from avocado; whole-wheat wrap adds more fiber. Salsas vary in sodium, so portion size matters.

Meal.name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Maple-Kissed Skillet
Meal.short_description: One-pan sheet-pan dinner: pork tenderloin roasted with potatoes and green beans in olive oil for a simple, balanced meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Toss potatoes and green beans with olive oil, salt, and pepper, and spread on a sheet pan. Rub pork tenderloin with herbs and a light coat of oil, place on the pan. Roast 25–30 minutes until the pork reaches 145°F (63°C) and the potatoes are tender. Rest pork 3–5 minutes, then slice and serve with the vegetables.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean pork tenderloin provides high-quality protein; potatoes offer complex carbohydrates; green beans add fiber and micronutrients; olive oil contributes heart-healthy fats. For a lighter version, use minimal added salt and portion appropriately. Let the pork rest after cooking to retain juiciness.

Meal.name: Roast Chicken Dinner — Garden Veg Combo
Meal.short_description: A balanced roast dinner with protein-rich chicken, wholesome quinoa, and colorful garden vegetables.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Roast chicken thighs (skinless if preferred), cook quinoa, and steam or roast broccoli and carrots. Plate together and finish with a squeeze of lemon and a light drizzle of olive oil for flavor.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein from chicken thighs; quinoa adds complete protein and fiber; broccoli and carrots provide vitamins, minerals, and additional fiber. For a leaner option, use skinless thighs.

Meal.name: Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Maple-Kissed Bake
Meal.short_description: Baked chicken with mixed vegetables and rice glazed in teriyaki sauce for a balanced, savory dinner.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Meal prep idea: bake a larger batch and portion into 4 servings. For more fiber, swap white rice for brown rice or quinoa. Opt for low-sodium teriyaki sauce or use less sauce. Add sesame seeds and green onions for flavor, and pair with a small side of fruit or leafy greens to enhance balance.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein from chicken supports satiety and muscle maintenance; mixed vegetables add fiber, vitamins, and minerals; rice provides energy from carbohydrates. Teriyaki sauce adds flavor but can raise sodium and added sugar; consider using low-sodium teriyaki or moderating sauce amount. For more fiber, substitute brown rice and/or increase vegetable portions.

Meal.name: Congee with Century Egg & Pork
Meal.short_description: Classic Chinese rice porridge simmered until silky, topped with sliced century egg, shredded pork, ginger, and scallions — a warm, easily digestible breakfast.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Simmer rice in broth until thick and creamy. Top with sliced century egg, shredded pork, julienned ginger, and chopped scallions. Add a drizzle of sesame oil and white pepper to taste. Pair with a side of Chinese cruller (youtiao) for dipping.
Meal.nutrition_note: Easily digestible rice porridge provides gentle carbohydrates; pork adds protein and iron; century egg contributes protein and B vitamins. Ginger aids digestion. Low in fat overall; sodium depends on broth choice — use low-sodium broth to control salt intake.

Meal.name: Japanese Tamagoyaki with Miso Soup
Meal.short_description: Rolled Japanese omelette (tamagoyaki) seasoned with dashi and mirin, served alongside a bowl of warm miso soup with tofu and wakame seaweed.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Whisk eggs with dashi, mirin, and a pinch of sugar; cook in a rectangular pan, rolling as you go. Serve with miso soup made from dashi stock, white miso paste, silken tofu cubes, and rehydrated wakame. Add steamed rice on the side for a traditional Japanese breakfast set.
Meal.nutrition_note: Eggs provide high-quality protein and choline; tofu adds plant-based protein and calcium; wakame seaweed offers iodine and minerals. Miso is fermented and may support gut health but is high in sodium — use reduced-sodium miso if watching salt. A balanced, light breakfast.

Meal.name: Vietnamese Bánh Mì Omelette
Meal.short_description: A fusion breakfast combining fluffy eggs with classic bánh mì fillings — pickled daikon, carrots, cilantro, and sriracha on a toasted baguette.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Cook a fluffy omelette and stuff into a lightly toasted baguette with quick-pickled daikon and carrot, fresh cilantro, cucumber slices, and a drizzle of sriracha or hoisin sauce. Pair with Vietnamese iced coffee for an authentic experience.
Meal.nutrition_note: Eggs supply protein; baguette provides carbohydrates for energy; pickled vegetables add fiber, probiotics, and vitamins A and C. Moderate in calories; sodium comes from pickled vegetables and sriracha — use sparingly if salt-sensitive.

Meal.name: Thai Basil Chicken (Pad Krapow) with Rice
Meal.short_description: Aromatic Thai stir-fry of ground chicken with holy basil, chilies, and garlic served over jasmine rice with a fried egg on top.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Stir-fry ground chicken with garlic, Thai chilies, and fish sauce until cooked, then toss in a generous handful of holy basil leaves. Serve over steamed jasmine rice topped with a crispy fried egg. Adjust chili to taste; pair with a side of cucumber slices to cool the palate.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean ground chicken provides protein with less fat than other meats; basil offers antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds; chilies may boost metabolism. Fish sauce is high in sodium — use measured amounts. Jasmine rice provides quick-digesting carbs; the fried egg adds extra protein and healthy fats.

Meal.name: Japanese Teriyaki Salmon Donburi
Meal.short_description: Pan-seared salmon fillet glazed with homemade teriyaki sauce, served over a bowl of steamed rice with steamed broccoli and pickled ginger.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Pan-sear salmon skin-side down until crispy, then glaze with a mix of soy sauce, mirin, and a touch of sugar. Serve over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and a small mound of pickled ginger. Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced nori.
Meal.nutrition_note: Salmon is rich in omega-3 fatty acids supporting heart and brain health; broccoli provides fiber, vitamin C, and sulforaphane; rice offers sustained energy. Teriyaki glaze contains sodium and sugar — make homemade to control amounts. Pickled ginger aids digestion.

Meal.name: Korean Bibimbap Bowl
Meal.short_description: A colorful Korean rice bowl topped with sautéed vegetables, seasoned beef, a fried egg, and spicy gochujang sauce — balanced and vibrant.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Arrange sautéed spinach, bean sprouts, carrots, and zucchini over steamed rice. Top with seasoned ground beef and a sunny-side-up egg. Serve with gochujang (Korean chili paste) mixed with sesame oil on the side. Mix everything together before eating for the best flavor.
Meal.nutrition_note: A well-rounded meal with protein from beef and egg, fiber and vitamins from assorted vegetables, and energy from rice. Gochujang contains capsaicin which may aid metabolism. Moderate in calories; control portions of rice and gochujang to manage carbs and sodium.

Meal.name: Mapo Tofu with Steamed Rice
Meal.short_description: A classic Sichuan dish of silken tofu braised in a spicy, numbing sauce with ground pork, doubanjiang, and Sichuan peppercorns — bold and comforting.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Brown ground pork, then stir in doubanjiang (fermented chili bean paste), minced garlic, and ginger. Add silken tofu cubes and simmer gently. Finish with Sichuan peppercorn powder and sliced scallions. Serve over steamed rice. Reduce chili paste for a milder version.
Meal.nutrition_note: Tofu provides plant-based protein, calcium, and isoflavones; pork adds complete protein and iron. Sichuan peppercorns have anti-inflammatory properties. Doubanjiang is high in sodium — use sparingly. A satisfying, protein-rich dish; pair with steamed greens for extra fiber.

Meal.name: Vietnamese Pho Bo (Beef Pho)
Meal.short_description: Aromatic Vietnamese beef noodle soup with a rich star anise and cinnamon-spiced broth, tender beef slices, and fresh rice noodles topped with herbs.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Simmer beef bones with star anise, cinnamon, cloves, and ginger for a fragrant broth. Serve over cooked rice noodles with thinly sliced beef (the hot broth cooks the raw slices). Garnish with bean sprouts, Thai basil, lime wedges, and sliced jalapeño. Add hoisin and sriracha to taste.
Meal.nutrition_note: Beef provides high-quality protein, iron, and zinc; rice noodles are gluten-free and easy to digest; bone broth may contain collagen and minerals. Fresh herbs and lime add vitamin C and antioxidants. Broth can be high in sodium — skim fat and use reduced-sodium broth if needed.

Meal.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod with Bok Choy
Meal.short_description: Buttery black cod marinated in sweet white miso glaze, broiled until caramelized, and served with garlic-sautéed bok choy and steamed rice.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Marinate black cod in a mixture of white miso, mirin, sake, and a touch of sugar for at least 2 hours (overnight is best). Broil until the glaze caramelizes. Sauté bok choy with garlic and a splash of soy sauce. Serve with steamed rice and garnish with sesame seeds.
Meal.nutrition_note: Black cod is exceptionally rich in omega-3 fatty acids and provides high-quality protein; bok choy offers calcium, vitamin C, and vitamin K. Miso is fermented and supports gut health but adds sodium. A nutrient-dense, elegant dinner; balance with vegetables and moderate rice portions.

Meal.name: Ricotta Toast — Sesame Set
Meal.short_description: Toasted whole-grain bread topped with ricotta, a drizzle of honey, and fresh berries.
Meal.meal_suggestion: A quick, balanced breakfast: toast with ricotta, honey, and berries; consider adding sesame seeds or nuts for extra fats and crunch, or pair with yogurt for more protein.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced: ricotta provides protein and calcium; whole-grain bread offers fiber; berries add antioxidants; honey adds natural sweetness; watch portions for calories; add sesame seeds if aligning with Sesame Set.

Meal.name: Cottage Cheese Bowl — Berry Boost Twist
Meal.short_description: A protein-packed cottage cheese bowl topped with pineapple and cinnamon, offering a light, refreshing breakfast or snack with tropical fruit sweetness.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Best served chilled as a quick breakfast or snack. For extra fiber and healthy fats, add a tablespoon of chia seeds or a small handful of nuts.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich cottage cheese supports satiety; pineapple supplies vitamin C and natural sweetness; cinnamon adds flavor with negligible calories and may offer minor blood sugar benefits.

Meal.name: Greek Yogurt Bowl — Garden Veg Bowl
Meal.short_description: Creamy yogurt bowl topped with berries, granola, and chia seeds—protein-rich, fiber-filled, and quick to enjoy.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Suggested serving: 1 cup Greek yogurt, 1/2 cup berries, 1/4 cup granola, 1 tsp chia seeds. For more veggie alignment, add a handful of spinach or cucumber on the side to echo the garden theme; or pair with a small side salad.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich Greek yogurt with calcium; berries add fiber and antioxidants; chia seeds supply omega-3s and extra fiber; granola contributes carbohydrates and crunch. Watch added sugar in granola; choose a low-sugar variety or portion-control.

Meal.name: Mediterranean Grain Bowl — Maple-Kissed Bake
Meal.short_description: A Mediterranean-inspired grain bowl with quinoa, chickpeas, cucumber, tomato, feta, and olive oil. This combination offers plant-based protein, fiber, calcium, and heart-healthy fats in a bright, fresh package.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve as a balanced bowl with quinoa and chickpeas as the base, topped with cucumber, tomato, feta, and a drizzle of olive oil. Add lemon juice and fresh herbs (parsley or mint) for brightness; great for meal prep when kept separate from dressings.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein- and fiber-rich due to quinoa and chickpeas; calcium from feta; healthy fats from olive oil; includes vitamins from cucumber and tomato. Moderately high in sodium from feta—substitute reduced-sodium feta or use less cheese to reduce sodium. Naturally gluten-free if quinoa is certified gluten-free; watch for cross-contamination.

Meal.name: Bean Burrito Bowl — Maple-Kissed Set
Meal.short_description: Bean Burrito Bowl with black beans, rice, corn, lettuce, salsa, and cheddar.
Meal.meal_suggestion: For a lighter option, use reduced-fat cheddar or dairy-free cheese; swap white rice for brown rice to boost fiber; add avocado or extra veggies for healthy fats and micronutrients.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced vegetarian bowl with plant protein from black beans and fiber from beans and rice; corn and salsa add micronutrients, while cheddar provides calcium and some saturated fat.

Meal.name: Bean Burrito Bowl — Berry Boost Combo
Meal.short_description: Bean burrito bowl with black beans, rice, corn, lettuce, salsa, and cheddar—protein- and fiber-rich with veggie toppings and a modest cheese portion.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Pair with fresh berries or a berry smoothie to honor the Berry Boost theme; consider adding avocado for healthy fats.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein- and fiber-rich from black beans and rice; calcium from cheddar; vitamins from salsa and lettuce; moderate fat depending on cheese amount. The 'Berry Boost' theme suggests adding berries or a berry side for extra antioxidants.

Meal.name: Chicken Fajita Bowl — Berry Boost Plate
Meal.short_description: Protein-forward fajita bowl with chicken, peppers, onions, rice, and avocado; optional berries boost antioxidants for a Berry Boost.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Grill or sauté chicken with peppers and onions, serve over cooked rice, and top with sliced avocado. Add a side of berries to boost antioxidants and fiber if desired.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich chicken with peppers and onions offers lean protein and fiber; rice provides carbohydrates; avocado adds heart-healthy fats. For extra fiber and antioxidants, consider adding a side of berries (Berry Boost).

Meal.name: Stuffed Bell Peppers — Classic Wrap
Meal.short_description: Protein-rich stuffed peppers with ground turkey, rice, tomato sauce, and cheddar in a colorful pepper shell.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve warm with a side salad or steamed greens. One pepper per serving; pair with salsa or Greek yogurt for extra creaminess, or swap rice for cauliflower rice to reduce carbs.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced: lean turkey provides protein, rice offers carbohydrates, and peppers add fiber; cheddar adds calcium and flavor. Monitor sodium from tomato sauce; lighter options include lean turkey, brown rice, and reduced-fat cheese.

Meal.name: Baked Salmon Plate — Berry Boost Twist
Meal.short_description: A baked salmon plate with lemon, vegetables, and rice, plus an optional Berry Boost Twist by adding berries for extra antioxidants.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Bake a 4–6 oz salmon fillet with lemon. Serve with 1 cup cooked rice and 1–2 cups mixed vegetables. For the Berry Boost Twist, add a handful of fresh berries on the side or a light berry glaze.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich salmon provides omega-3 fats (EPA/DHA) and high-quality protein. Mixed vegetables supply fiber and micronutrients; lemon adds vitamin C. Rice contributes carbohydrates for energy; brown rice can boost fiber. Berry Boost Twist suggests adding berries (blueberries, strawberries, etc.) for antioxidant benefits.

Meal.name: Oatmeal — Smoky Variation
Meal.short_description: Creamy milk-based oatmeal with banana and almonds, offering a balanced, fiber-rich start to the day with a subtle smoky variation.
Meal.meal_suggestion: A warm, smoky-flavored oatmeal with banana and almonds. Serve hot; for extra depth, add a pinch of cinnamon or a light drizzle of honey or maple syrup.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced breakfast with complex carbs (oats), protein and calcium (milk), potassium and natural sweetness (banana), and healthy fats/protein (almonds). Optional adjustments: use skim milk for lower calories or whole milk for more creaminess.

Meal.name: Overnight Oats — Mild-Spice Set
Meal.short_description: Creamy overnight oats with apple and cinnamon—a fiber- and protein-rich, mildly spiced breakfast.
Meal.meal_suggestion: For more protein, mix in Greek yogurt or a protein powder. Boost fiber and omega-3s with chia or flax seeds. Top with extra diced apple or a pinch more cinnamon. To make dairy-free, use plant milk and yogurt alternatives.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced mix of fiber (oats, apple), protein and calcium (milk and yogurt); cinnamon adds flavor with minimal sugar. To lower sugar, use unsweetened dairy or dairy-free alternatives and skip any additional sweeteners.

Meal.name: Whole-Grain Pancakes — Lemon-Herb Skillet
Meal.short_description: Whole-grain pancakes with eggs and milk, topped with berries and a light maple sweetness.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Pair with a side of yogurt or extra berries to boost protein and fiber; limit maple syrup to 1–2 tsp or swap for a berry compote; add lemon zest or fresh herbs to echo the lemon-herb concept without adding sugar.
Meal.nutrition_note: Whole-wheat flour provides fiber; eggs and milk contribute protein and calcium; berries supply vitamins and antioxidants; maple syrup adds sugars—use sparingly or pair with extra fruit to moderate sweetness.

Meal.name: Black Bean Wrap — Garden Veg Skillet
Meal.short_description: A fiber-rich, plant-based wrap featuring black beans, avocado, lettuce, and salsa in a whole-wheat tortilla.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Great quick lunch or light dinner. Enhance protein with a dollop of Greek yogurt or sprinkle of nutritional yeast, and add more veggies or a side salad for balance.
Meal.nutrition_note: Plant-based, high in fiber and plant protein from black beans; healthy fats from avocado; whole-wheat wrap adds more fiber. Salsa and lettuce contribute volume with relatively low calories. Watch sodium if using store-bought salsa.

Meal.name: Chicken Rice Bowl — Maple-Kissed Variation
Meal.short_description: A simple chicken rice bowl with broccoli and soy seasoning—protein, carbs, and vegetables in one dish, with mindful sodium and potential maple variation.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Grill or sauté chicken, steam broccoli, and serve over cooked rice. Lightly drizzle with soy sauce. For a lower-sodium option, use low-sodium soy sauce and consider brown rice for more fiber.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein from chicken, carbs from rice, and fiber from broccoli create a balanced plate. Soy sauce adds sodium; the Maple-Kissed name hints at sweetness not present in the listed ingredients, so adding maple would change the nutrition profile.

Meal.name: Soba Noodle Bowl — Berry Boost Variation
Meal.short_description: Chilled soba noodles with cucumber, carrot, and egg, tossed in sesame dressing.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve cold or at room temperature; consider adding berries to reflect the Berry Boost variation and/or increase protein with extra egg or tofu; adjust dressing for desired fat content.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced meal with complex carbs from soba, protein from egg, and fiber/micronutrients from cucumber and carrot. Sesame dressing provides healthy fats; the Berry Boost label isn’t represented by these ingredients unless berries are added.

Meal.name: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Garden Veg Variation
Meal.short_description: A plant-forward combination of vegetable soup, whole grain bread, and a side salad for fiber, nutrients, and steady energy.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve 1 cup of vegetable soup with 1 to 2 slices of whole grain bread and a side salad. For extra protein, add beans, lentils, or tofu to the soup or include a protein-rich topping on the salad.
Meal.nutrition_note: Plant-forward, fiber-rich meal with vegetables, whole grains, and greens. High in dietary fiber and vitamins; provides moderate protein from soup and bread. To keep sodium in check, choose a low-sodium vegetable soup. Optional protein boost (beans, lentils, or tofu) can be added for more protein.

Meal.name: Stuffed Bell Peppers — Citrus Fresh Combo
Meal.short_description: Bell peppers stuffed with lean turkey, rice, tomato sauce, and cheddar—a balanced mix of protein, starch, and vegetables.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve one stuffed pepper per person with a simple side salad. For a lighter version, use brown rice and reduced-fat cheddar; choose low-sodium tomato sauce. Bake until peppers are tender.
Meal.nutrition_note: High in protein from lean ground turkey; includes complex carbs from rice and fiber from peppers. Cheddar adds fat and calcium. Tomato sauce provides lycopene; to manage sodium, use low-sodium sauce and consider brown rice for extra fiber.

Meal.name: DIY Taco Night — Garden Veg Wrap
Meal.short_description: DIY Taco Night Garden Veg Wrap: lean ground beef, corn tortillas, lettuce, tomato, cheddar, and salsa wrapped into a protein-rich, veggie-forward option.
Meal.meal_suggestion: To boost nutrition: use whole-grain tortillas, add a bean or avocado side, and load with extra veggies. Choose low-sodium salsa and cheese or reduce cheese. Serve with a simple side salad or fruit.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich meal from lean ground beef and cheddar; includes veggies (lettuce, tomato) and carbs from corn tortillas. Moderate sodium from cheese and salsa. For more fiber and heart-healthy fats, swap to whole-grain tortillas and add beans or avocado, and opt for lower-sodium cheese/salsa.

Meal.name: Oatmeal — Garden Veg Skillet
Meal.short_description: Creamy skillet oatmeal with milk, banana, and almonds—fiber and protein-rich breakfast with natural sweetness.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Warm skillet-style oatmeal with milk, banana, and almonds; top with cinnamon or berries. To honor the 'Garden Veg Skillet' concept, serve with sautéed greens on the side or fold in finely chopped spinach into the oats.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich oats provide steady energy; milk adds protein and calcium; almonds contribute heart-healthy fats and extra protein; banana supplies potassium and natural sweetness. This meal offers a balanced macro profile with filling fiber and protein.

Meal.name: Egg Scramble — Sesame Bake
Meal.short_description: A quick egg scramble with spinach and tomatoes sautéed in olive oil. Protein-rich, veggie-forward, and dairy-free.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Warm and ready in minutes; enjoy as a breakfast or light lunch. Consider serving with a slice of whole-grain toast for extra fiber.
Meal.nutrition_note: High-quality protein from eggs and healthy fats from olive oil support satiety. Spinach provides iron, folate, and vitamin K; tomatoes offer vitamin C and lycopene. Sesame is not used in this recipe.

Meal.name: Ricotta Toast — Citrus Fresh Bake
Meal.short_description: Open-faced whole-grain toast topped with ricotta, fresh berries, and a light honey drizzle with a citrusy finish.
Meal.meal_suggestion: A quick, balanced breakfast or snack. For extra brightness, add lemon/orange zest or segments; pair with yogurt or a handful of nuts if more protein and fats are desired.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein and calcium from ricotta; fiber from whole-grain bread; antioxidants from berries; natural sweetness from honey. Moderately high in sugars; suitable for a light meal or snack. For dairy-free, use plant-based ricotta.

Meal.name: Black Bean Wrap — Garlic-Ginger Wrap
Meal.short_description: A hearty plant-based wrap with black beans, avocado, lettuce, and salsa in a whole-wheat tortilla.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with a side of fruit and, if desired, dairy-free yogurt for extra protein. For lower sodium, use low-sodium salsa and adjust wrap/bean portions; to boost protein, add an extra handful of beans or a small serving of quinoa on the side.
Meal.nutrition_note: Plant-based protein and fiber from black beans; healthy fats from avocado; plus complex carbs from whole-wheat wrap. Provides vitamins A/C and potassium from lettuce and salsa. Watch sodium from salsa; choose low-sodium or control portion. Garlic and ginger can be added for flavor and potential anti-inflammatory benefits.

Meal.name: Tuna Pasta Salad — Garlic-Ginger Plate
Meal.short_description: Tuna pasta salad with cucumber, cherry tomatoes, and olive oil.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve chilled as a light main. For more fiber and flavor, swap to whole-grain pasta and add lemon juice and fresh herbs; optional garlic or ginger can be added if desired.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein from tuna and carbs from pasta provide satiety; olive oil adds healthy fats; cucumber and cherry tomatoes boost fiber and micronutrients. Note: canned tuna can be high in sodium; consider rinsing or choosing low-sodium options. The name mentions garlic/ginger, but they are not in the listed ingredients.

Meal.name: Tuna Pasta Salad — Mild-Spice Plate
Meal.short_description: A simple tuna pasta salad with cucumber, cherry tomatoes, and olive oil—protein-forward, veggie-rich, and mildly flavored for lunch or as a side.
Meal.meal_suggestion: This dish works as a light main or side. Serve about 1 cup cooked pasta with tuna and chopped cucumber and tomatoes, dressed with olive oil and a squeeze of lemon or herbs for mild flavor. For more fiber, swap to whole-grain pasta; add leafy greens on the side.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich tuna provides lean protein (~20–25 g per serving). Pasta supplies carbohydrates; olive oil adds heart-healthy fats. Cucumber and cherry tomatoes add fiber and micronutrients. Watch sodium from canned tuna; opt tuna in water and rinse, and consider whole-grain pasta for extra fiber.

Meal.name: Chicken Stir-Fry — Garlic-Ginger Skillet
Meal.short_description: Quick chicken and vegetable stir-fry over brown rice with a garlic-soy glaze.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Keep portions balanced (4–6 oz chicken, 1 cup vegetables, 1 cup cooked brown rice) and consider low-sodium soy sauce or a splash of citrus to reduce sodium.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean protein from chicken; fiber and micronutrients from brown rice and vegetables; moderate sodium from soy sauce. Garlic provides flavor with potential health benefits. For lower sodium, use low-sodium soy sauce.

Meal.name: Teriyaki Chicken Dinner — Berry Boost Set
Meal.short_description: Teriyaki chicken with vegetables and rice; a simple, protein-rich meal with moderate carbs. Sauce adds flavor but also sodium/sugar; adding a berry side can boost antioxidants.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Assemble a bowl with 4–6 oz cooked chicken, 1 cup cooked rice, and 1.5–2 cups mixed vegetables. Drizzle teriyaki sauce to taste. For a Berry Boost, include a side of berries (strawberries, blueberries) to boost antioxidants.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean protein from chicken, fiber and micronutrients from mixed vegetables, and carbohydrates from rice create a balanced plate. Teriyaki sauce adds sodium and sugars; using a lower-sodium option or reducing sauce helps. Berry Boost isn’t reflected by the listed ingredients—adding berries would increase antioxidants and fiber.

Meal.name: Tofu Veggie Curry — Smoky Bake
Meal.short_description: Smoky baked tofu with mixed vegetables in curry sauce, served over rice.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Best as a quick weeknight meal. Typical serving: 1 cup cooked rice with a generous portion of tofu-vegetable curry. For more protein, add beans; to reduce carbs, swap rice for cauliflower rice. Leftovers keep 3-4 days in the fridge.
Meal.nutrition_note: Plant-based protein from tofu provides essential amino acids; fiber from vegetables and rice supports fullness. Sodium may be high if you use store-bought curry sauce—consider a low-sodium option or homemade curry to reduce salt. Baking tofu with minimal oil keeps fat modest while still adding flavor.

Meal.name: Banana Bread Toast — Lemon-Herb Bake
Meal.short_description: Banana bread toast topped with Greek yogurt and berries — a balanced mix of carbs, protein, and fiber.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Toast a slice of banana bread, spread on Greek yogurt, and top with fresh berries for a protein-rich, fiber-forward breakfast or snack with steady energy.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein from Greek yogurt; carbs from banana bread; fiber and antioxidants from berries. A quick, balanced meal; consider a smaller bread slice or lower-fat yogurt to manage calories if needed.

Meal.name: Oatmeal — Classic Combo
Meal.short_description: Creamy oat porridge with banana and almonds—a quick, balanced breakfast with fiber, protein, and healthy fats.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Enjoy warm as oatmeal; top with banana slices and chopped almonds. For dairy-free options, use fortified plant milk. Add cinnamon or berries for extra flavor; can be prepared as overnight oats.
Meal.nutrition_note: A balanced, nutrient-dense breakfast: complex carbs from oats, protein and calcium from milk, healthy fats from almonds, plus fiber and potassium from banana. Calorie content varies with milk type and portion size.

Meal.name: Fruit & Nut Plate — Berry Boost Bowl
Meal.short_description: Yogurt with mixed fruit and nuts—a quick, balanced Berry Boost Bowl.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve as a bowl: 1 cup mixed fruit, 1/2 cup yogurt (Greek yogurt optional for extra protein), and a small handful of nuts. Optional toppings: cinnamon, chia seeds. Pair with water or tea.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced option: protein from yogurt, fiber and micronutrients from mixed fruit, and healthy fats from nuts. For lower added sugar, use plain yogurt and keep nut portions modest.

Meal.name: Salmon Salad Wrap — Garden Veg Bake
Meal.short_description: Protein-packed salmon salad with yogurt dressing, celery and greens wrapped in a whole-wheat tortilla for a balanced lunch.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve as a cold wrap for portability; for the Garden Veg Bake version, bake the salmon-yogurt mix with roasted vegetables briefly, then wrap or serve over greens.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich (canned salmon, Greek yogurt) with omega-3s; fiber from greens and celery; whole-wheat tortilla adds complex carbs; contains dairy; watch sodium from canned salmon.

Meal.name: Veggie Quesadilla — Garlic-Ginger Skillet
Meal.short_description: A quick skillet veggie quesadilla on a whole-wheat tortilla with cheddar, bell pepper, spinach, and salsa.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Pair with fruit or a simple bean salad to boost fiber and protein; consider adding avocado for healthy fats or using reduced-fat cheese to lower fat content.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich whole-wheat tortilla and vegetables provide dietary fiber. Cheddar adds protein and calcium. Spinach and peppers supply vitamins A and C. Moderate fat from cheese; sodium level depends on salsa and cheese amount. For a lighter version, choose reduced-fat cheese or use less cheese.

Meal.name: Chicken Caesar Wrap — Classic Style
Meal.short_description: A classic Caesar-inspired wrap with chicken, romaine, parmesan, and light Caesar dressing wrapped in a whole-wheat tortilla.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Great quick lunch option. Pair with fruit or veggie sticks; to boost fiber, add extra veggies or use a higher-fiber wrap, and consider reduced-sodium dressing to lower sodium intake.
Meal.nutrition_note: Estimated per-wrap: 350–450 kcal, 25–35 g protein, 5–8 g fiber, 10–15 g fat; sodium approximately 500–800 mg. Provides lean protein and fiber; dressing and cheese contribute to fat and sodium.

Meal.name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Classic Set
Meal.short_description: A quick sheet-pan dinner featuring pork tenderloin, potatoes, and green beans tossed in olive oil and herbs.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with a simple green salad or steamed greens to boost fiber. Season with herbs like rosemary or thyme. For more fiber, swap half the potatoes for sweet potatoes. Leftovers keep well in the fridge for 3–4 days.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean pork tenderloin provides high-quality protein with minimal fat; potatoes supply complex carbs and potassium; green beans add fiber and micronutrients; olive oil delivers heart-healthy fats. For a standard 1-serving portion (about 4–6 oz pork, 1 cup potatoes, 1 cup green beans, 1 tbsp olive oil) expect roughly 30–35 g protein, 35–45 g carbs, 12–15 g fat (~400–500 kcal). To lower calories, use 1 tsp oil or load more veggies; to increase fiber, add a leafy side.

Meal.name: Baked Cod Plate — Sesame Twist
Meal.short_description: Baked cod with roasted sweet potato and broccoli, finished with lemon for a bright, protein-rich plate.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with quinoa or brown rice for extra fiber. If desired, garnish with sesame seeds or a light sesame oil drizzle to mirror the twist.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean cod provides high-quality protein; sweet potato offers complex carbs and beta-carotene; broccoli adds fiber and micronutrients; lemon contributes vitamin C. Optional sesame seeds/oil can add healthy fats to match the 'Sesame Twist'.

Meal.name: Turkey Meatballs & Pasta — Mild-Spice Set
Meal.short_description: Lean turkey meatballs with whole-wheat pasta in tomato sauce, finished with parmesan for calcium and a mild spice profile.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve with steamed greens (e.g., broccoli or spinach) to add color and fiber. A typical portion: pasta with turkey meatballs in marinara, topped with parmesan.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean turkey provides protein; whole-wheat pasta offers fiber and complex carbs; parmesan adds calcium. Tomato marinara adds micronutrients but can be high in sodium—use low-sodium sauce and moderate cheese for a lighter option; pair with vegetables for balance.

Meal.name: Miso Breakfast Set — Garlic-Ginger Bake
Meal.short_description: Garlic-ginger miso bake featuring tofu, egg, green onion, and rice—a savory, protein-rich breakfast.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve warm and pair with steamed greens for extra fiber and micronutrients. To tailor, use brown rice or a low-sodium miso variant; vegan option: omit egg or replace with a plant-based protein.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich breakfast (tofu + egg) with rice as the carb base. Miso provides flavor but is high in sodium, so consider low-sodium miso or reducing amount. Contains soy and egg; gluten content depends on miso type (choose gluten-free miso if needed). For more fiber, swap white rice for brown rice.

Meal.name: Fruit & Nut Plate — Citrus Fresh Style
Meal.short_description: A citrus-inspired Fruit & Nut Plate with yogurt, mixed fruit, and nuts for protein, healthy fats, and fiber.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Enjoy as a quick snack or light breakfast: a bowl of yogurt topped with mixed fruit and a small handful of nuts; finish with a squeeze of citrus juice or zest for a bright, refreshing kick.
Meal.nutrition_note: Yogurt provides protein and calcium; nuts contribute healthy fats and satiety; fruit adds fiber, vitamins, and natural sweetness. For a lighter option, choose plain yogurt and unsalted nuts, and add citrus segments for extra freshness.

Meal.name: Greek Yogurt Bowl — Lemon-Herb Variation
Meal.short_description: A protein-packed yogurt bowl with berries, granola, and chia seeds, lightly flavored with lemon and herbs for a refreshing breakfast or snack.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Assemble in a bowl; sprinkle lemon zest and chopped fresh herbs (e.g., mint or basil) to emphasize the lemon-herb variation. Consider a smaller portion of granola to reduce calories, and add a splash of lemon juice for extra tang if desired.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich Greek yogurt supports satiety and muscle recovery. Berries and chia seeds add fiber and omega-3s; granola provides crunch and carbohydrates. Choose a low-sugar granola to keep sugar in check and use lemon zest/herbs to flavor without added sugar.

Meal.name: Tuna Pasta Salad — Classic Skillet
Meal.short_description: A quick skillet-style pasta salad with tuna, cucumber, cherry tomatoes, and olive oil. Protein-forward, with fresh veggies and healthy fats for a simple, balanced meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Use whole-grain pasta for extra fiber, and if available add more vegetables (e.g., spinach, peppers) and a squeeze of lemon for brightness. Keep olive oil portions moderate to manage calories.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein from tuna and olive oil provide healthy fats and satiety; pasta offers energy. Cucumber and cherry tomatoes add fiber and micronutrients. For more fiber, use whole-grain pasta; if sodium is a concern, rinse tuna or choose tuna packed in water.

Meal.name: Lentil Soup — Sesame Variation
Meal.short_description: Lentil soup with carrots, celery, and onion in broth, served with whole-grain bread; sesame variation optional.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve hot with a slice of toasted whole-grain bread. Optional sesame garnish can be added for extra flavor and nutrients.
Meal.nutrition_note: Plant-based protein and fiber from lentils; vegetables provide vitamins/minerals; whole-grain bread adds fiber and complex carbs. Broth-based soup tends to be low in saturated fat, with sodium depending on the stock used. If sesame variation is included, it adds healthy fats and calcium.

Meal.name: Hummus Snack Plate — Berry Boost Twist
Meal.short_description: A quick, balanced snack plate with protein from hummus and cheese, fiber from whole-wheat pita and fresh veggies (cucumber and cherry tomatoes).
Meal.meal_suggestion: Enjoy as a quick snack plate; to align with the Berry Boost Twist, add a handful of berries on the side.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced protein from hummus and cheese; fiber from whole-wheat pita and veggies; healthy fats from hummus. Berry Boost Twist not represented by the current ingredients; add berries for antioxidants if desired. Typical portions: 1–2 pita wedges, 2–3 tbsp hummus, 1 oz cheese, veggies to taste.

Meal.name: Vegetable Soup & Bread — Citrus Fresh Twist
Meal.short_description: A light, satisfying trio of vegetable soup, whole-grain bread, and a side salad with a citrus-inspired twist.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Balanced light lunch option. For extra protein, add chickpeas, lentils, or a dollop of yogurt. To enhance the Citrus Fresh Twist, consider a citrus vinaigrette on the salad.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich from the vegetables and whole-grain bread; provides complex carbs, and vitamins/minerals with low saturated fat. Protein is modest; sodium depends on the soup—opt for a low-sodium option to support heart health. Side salad adds hydration and micronutrients.

Meal.name: Roast Chicken Dinner — Smoky Plate
Meal.short_description: Smoky roast chicken thighs served with quinoa and roasted broccoli and carrots—a protein- and fiber-rich dinner.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Aim for a balanced plate: half vegetables (broccoli and carrots), one-quarter protein (chicken), one-quarter quinoa. For leaner fat, use skinless thighs; boost flavor with smoked paprika or chipotle. Drizzle a small amount of olive oil during roasting for healthy fats.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich meal from chicken thighs with quinoa provides complete protein and fiber. Broccoli and carrots add vitamins A, C, and K along with potassium. Moderate fat from thighs; skinless options reduce fat. Gluten-free and naturally balanced for a dinner.

Meal.name: Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Berry Boost Skillet
Meal.short_description: One-pan sheet-pan pork tenderloin with potatoes and green beans, seasoned with olive oil and herbs.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Roast on a sheet pan: toss pork tenderloin (whole or sliced into medallions), potato chunks, and trimmed green beans with olive oil, salt, pepper, and herbs (rosemary or thyme). Arrange on a single pan and roast at 425°F (220°C) for 25–30 minutes, until the pork reaches 145–150°F (63–66°C) and potatoes are tender. Rest briefly, then slice pork and serve with the vegetables.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean pork tenderloin offers high-quality protein; potatoes provide energy-rich complex carbohydrates; green beans contribute fiber and micronutrients; olive oil supplies heart-healthy fats. This one-pan approach supports balanced portions and easier cleanup; adjust portions to fit dietary needs.

Meal.name: Ricotta Toast — Garden Veg Variation
Meal.short_description: Fiber-rich ricotta toast topped with berries and a touch of honey for a balanced breakfast or snack.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Toast whole-grain bread, spread with ricotta, drizzle with a small amount of honey, and top with fresh berries. Garden Veg Variation: add thin cucumber slices, tomato wedges, or a handful of greens under the ricotta for extra vegetables.
Meal.nutrition_note: Whole-grain bread provides fiber and sustained energy; ricotta adds protein and calcium; berries deliver vitamins and antioxidants; honey contributes natural sweetness. Suitable as a balanced breakfast or snack; for lower sugar, reduce honey portion.

Meal.name: Smoothie — Smoky Variation
Meal.short_description: Creamy banana peanut butter oat smoothie; a protein- and fiber-rich option with potential smoky flavor tweaks.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serving idea: enjoy as a balanced breakfast or post-workout snack. To adjust macros, use dairy-free milk or lower-fat milk; to boost protein, add Greek yogurt or a protein powder; to introduce a smoky note, blend in a pinch of smoked paprika or a dash of cinnamon.
Meal.nutrition_note: Creamy smoothie built from banana, milk, peanut butter and oats. Rich in protein from peanut butter and milk, with fiber from banana and oats, and healthy fats from peanut butter. Calorie content varies by milk type and portion, but a typical 12–16 oz serving is about 400–480 kcal with roughly 14–22 g protein, 40–60 g carbohydrates and 12–20 g fat.

Meal.name: Greek Yogurt Bowl — Garden Veg Style
Meal.short_description: A creamy Greek yogurt bowl with berries, granola, and chia seeds offering protein, fiber, and healthy fats; easily adaptable to a garden-vegetable theme with a simple veggie garnish.
Meal.meal_suggestion: To emphasize the Garden Veg Style, add a small side of chopped cucumber or tomato or top with fresh herbs. For a veggie boost, stir in a handful of spinach or arugula before serving. Opt for lower-sugar granola if desired.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich Greek yogurt provides calcium and probiotics; berries add fiber and antioxidants; chia seeds contribute omega-3 fats and extra fiber; granola adds complex carbohydrates and crunch. If watching sugar, choose a lower-sugar granola.

Meal.name: Veggie Quesadilla — Smoky Combo
Meal.short_description: Veggie quesadilla with a whole-wheat tortilla, cheddar, bell pepper, spinach, and salsa for a smoky, veggie-forward meal.
Meal.meal_suggestion: To lighten it, use reduced-fat cheddar or part-skim cheese; serve with a side of fruit or a simple bean salad for extra fiber and protein.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber from the whole-wheat tortilla and vegetables; calcium and protein from cheddar; moderate fat from cheese; sodium may be elevated from cheddar and salsa.

Meal.name: Veggie Quesadilla — Sesame Wrap
Meal.short_description: A warm veggie quesadilla on a whole-wheat tortilla filled with cheddar, bell pepper, spinach, and salsa.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Pair with a side of fruit or a light yogurt. For more protein in a vegetarian option, add black beans or use bean-based alternative; consider reduced-fat cheddar to lower fat. Optional additions: avocado for healthy fats or a green salad to increase volume and micronutrients.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-wheat tortilla and vegetables; calcium from cheddar; iron from spinach; vitamins A and C from bell pepper. Moderate sodium from cheese and salsa. To reduce fat and calories, use reduced-fat cheddar and a smaller amount of salsa.

Meal.name: Salmon Salad Wrap — Classic Wrap
Meal.short_description: A classic Salmon Salad Wrap made with canned salmon, Greek yogurt, celery, greens, and a whole-wheat tortilla.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Great for a portable lunch. Use one whole-wheat tortilla and fill with the salmon-yogurt mixture and greens; pair with fruit or veggie sticks for balance. For make-ahead prep, store the filling separately and assemble the wrap just before eating to prevent sogginess.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-packed and rich in omega-3s from salmon and Greek yogurt. Includes fiber from greens, celery, and whole-wheat tortilla. Be mindful of sodium from canned salmon; opt for low-sodium salmon and yogurt if you monitor salt intake.

Meal.name: Shrimp Fried Rice — Lemon-Herb Combo
Meal.short_description: Stir-fried shrimp, egg, peas, carrots, and rice with soy sauce—protein-packed, veggie-rich, and easily customizable with whole-grain rice or lower-sodium seasoning.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Balanced quick meal. To reduce sodium, use low-sodium soy sauce; for more fiber, use brown rice; add a squeeze of lemon and fresh herbs to emphasize the lemon-herb note.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-rich from shrimp and egg; includes veggies (peas, carrots) and carbs from rice. Soy sauce adds sodium, so consider low-sodium soy sauce or tamari. For more fiber, swap white rice for brown rice; gluten-free option with a gluten-free soy sauce. Lemon zest/herbs can boost flavor without many calories.

Meal.name: Homemade Pizza Night — Garden Veg Combo
Meal.short_description: Homemade garden-veggie pizza with whole-wheat crust, tomato sauce, mozzarella, mushrooms, and bell pepper—colorful and fiber-rich.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve a personal 8–12 inch pizza with a side salad. For a lighter version, use part-skim mozzarella and low-sodium tomato sauce, and consider adding more vegetables or a lean protein if desired.
Meal.nutrition_note: Balanced: whole-wheat crust (fiber) + mozzarella (protein/calcium) + veggies (vitamins/minerals). Carbs mainly from crust; monitor sodium from cheese/sauce; consider portion control and optional low-sodium ingredients or extra veggies for fiber.

Meal.name: DIY Taco Night — Mild-Spice Style
Meal.short_description: A customizable DIY taco night featuring lean beef, corn tortillas, fresh veggies, cheddar, and salsa with mild spice.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Cook lean ground beef with mild taco seasoning; warm corn tortillas; assemble with lettuce, tomato, cheddar, and salsa. For extra fiber and fats, add beans or avocado. Typical portion: 1–2 tortillas, ~4 oz beef, generous veggies, 1/4 cup cheese.
Meal.nutrition_note: Lean beef provides high-quality protein; corn tortillas supply carbohydrates and some fiber; lettuce and tomato add vitamins and additional fiber; cheddar contributes calcium and fat. Monitor sodium from salsa and seasoning—use low-sodium salsa and moderate cheese, and consider adding beans or avocado to boost fiber and heart-healthy fats.

Meal.name: Overnight Oats — Berry Boost Variation
Meal.short_description: Creamy overnight oats with apple and cinnamon—a protein- and fiber-rich breakfast.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Mix ingredients to make overnight oats: rolled oats, milk, plain yogurt, diced apple, and cinnamon. Refrigerate overnight and enjoy cold or warmed.
Meal.nutrition_note: A balanced, protein- and fiber-rich breakfast. Oats provide soluble fiber; yogurt adds protein and calcium; apple contributes vitamins and extra fiber. Cinnamon adds flavor with minimal calories and may help with blood sugar regulation.

Meal.name: Smoothie — Lemon-Herb Style
Meal.short_description: Creamy banana-milk smoothie with peanut butter and oats; balanced protein, carbs, and fats with fiber.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Creamy, protein-forward smoothie great for breakfast or post-workout. For a lemon-herb twist, blend in lemon zest and a few fresh herbs (mint or basil) with the listed ingredients.
Meal.nutrition_note: Approx. 550–600 kcal per serving. Protein ~22–28 g; carbs ~60–70 g; fat ~22–28 g. Rich in potassium (banana), calcium/vitamin D (milk), and fiber (oats). Note: the lemon-herb flavor is not represented by the current ingredients; to add lemon/herb notes, blend in lemon zest and fresh herbs (e.g., mint or basil) — this will adjust macros slightly.

Meal.name: Granola & Milk — Garlic-Ginger Plate
Meal.short_description: A quick bowl combining granola, milk, and banana for a simple breakfast or snack.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Keep as 1 serving granola + 1 cup milk + 1 medium banana. For more protein, use higher-protein milk or add yogurt; to reduce sugar, choose unsweetened granola. If you want a savory nod to the name, add a tiny pinch of minced garlic and grated ginger, but use sparingly as this will alter flavor and nutrition.
Meal.nutrition_note: Carbs from granola and banana; protein and calcium from milk. May be high in added sugars if granola is sweetened; consider unsweetened granola or smaller portions and add fiber with berries or seeds.

Meal.name: Caprese Sandwich — Maple-Kissed Variation
Meal.short_description: Caprese-inspired sandwich on whole-grain bread with mozzarella, tomato, basil, and balsamic; maple-kissed variation optional with a light maple glaze.
Meal.meal_suggestion: A quick, balanced lunch: Caprese sandwich on whole-grain bread with mozzarella, tomato, basil, and balsamic. For the maple-kissed twist, drizzle a small amount of maple-balsamic glaze.
Meal.nutrition_note: Includes fiber from whole-grain bread and protein plus calcium from mozzarella; tomatoes and basil supply vitamins and antioxidants; balsamic adds flavor with modest sugars. A maple-kissed variation can use a light maple-balsamic glaze to introduce sweetness without heavy sugar load.

Meal.name: Lentil Soup — Citrus Fresh Set
Meal.short_description: A hearty, plant-based lentil soup with vegetables served alongside whole-grain bread—protein- and fiber-rich for a vegetarian lunch or dinner.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve hot with warm whole-grain bread; brighten with a squeeze of lemon or lemon zest to echo the Citrus Fresh theme.
Meal.nutrition_note: Plant-based protein and fiber from lentils, plus vitamins/minerals from carrots, celery, and onion. Whole-grain bread adds extra fiber and complex carbs. For lower sodium, use a low-sodium broth; a squeeze of lemon can boost citrus brightness.

Meal.name: Chicken Noodle Soup — Citrus Fresh Twist
Meal.short_description: A comforting chicken noodle soup with carrots and celery in broth, offering protein, vegetables, and warmth with a suggested citrus brightness.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Finish with lemon zest or juice to emphasize the citrus twist; for more fiber, use whole-grain noodles and pair with a small fruit serving for extra vitamin C.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein from chicken and carbohydrates from noodles provide balanced nutrition; carrots and celery add fiber and micronutrients; using a light broth helps keep fat moderate. The citrus twist is not shown in the listed ingredients, so lemon would boost vitamin C if added.

Meal.name: Creamy Tomato Soup with Grilled Cheese Dippers
Meal.short_description: A kid-friendly classic — velvety tomato soup served with crispy whole-wheat grilled cheese strips for dunking.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil, add canned crushed tomatoes and a splash of milk or cream, then blend until smooth. Season with basil, salt, and pepper. Serve in a bowl alongside whole-wheat grilled cheese cut into sticks for easy dipping. A drizzle of olive oil on top adds richness.
Meal.nutrition_note: Tomatoes are rich in lycopene and vitamin C; whole-wheat bread provides fiber and complex carbs; cheddar adds calcium and protein. Milk contributes additional calcium. Keep cream or milk portions moderate to manage fat; using part-skim cheese or reduced-fat milk can lighten the dish while preserving flavor.

Meal.name: Teriyaki Chicken Rice Bowl
Meal.short_description: Sweet and savory teriyaki-glazed chicken over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and shredded carrots — colorful, balanced, and easy to eat.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Pan-sear bite-sized chicken pieces until golden, then toss in a homemade teriyaki glaze (soy sauce, honey, garlic, ginger). Serve over steamed rice with steamed broccoli florets and shredded carrots on the side. Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced scallions for a restaurant-style touch.
Meal.nutrition_note: Chicken breast provides lean protein; broccoli is rich in vitamin C, vitamin K, and fiber; carrots add beta-carotene. Rice supplies energy from carbohydrates. Homemade teriyaki lets you control sugar and sodium — use low-sodium soy sauce and moderate honey to keep it balanced.

Meal.name: Mini Meatball Pasta Bake
Meal.short_description: Tender turkey meatballs baked with whole-wheat penne in marinara sauce and topped with melted mozzarella — hearty comfort food with a nutritious twist.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Mix ground turkey with breadcrumbs, egg, garlic, and Italian seasoning; roll into small meatballs and bake at 400°F for 15 minutes. Toss cooked whole-wheat penne with marinara sauce and the meatballs in a baking dish, top with shredded mozzarella, and bake 10 more minutes until bubbly. Serve with a side of steamed green beans or a small salad.
Meal.nutrition_note: Ground turkey is a leaner alternative to beef, providing protein with less saturated fat; whole-wheat pasta adds fiber for sustained energy; marinara sauce supplies lycopene from tomatoes. Mozzarella provides calcium. Watch portion sizes of cheese and pasta to manage calories; pair with vegetables for a more complete meal.

Meal.name: Whole-Grain Pancakes — Smoky Plate
Meal.short_description: Whole-wheat pancakes with egg and milk, topped with berries and maple syrup.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Pair with a small serving of Greek yogurt or a handful of nuts to boost protein and healthy fats; use a modest amount of maple syrup to keep added sugars in check.
Meal.nutrition_note: Fiber-rich from whole-wheat flour; good protein from egg and milk; berries provide vitamins and antioxidants. Maple syrup adds sugars, so portion control is helpful. Contains dairy and gluten.

Meal.name: Avocado Toast — Garlic-Ginger Combo
Meal.short_description: Avocado toast on whole-grain bread topped with egg and a lemon finish.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Toast the bread, mash avocado and spread on top, add a cooked egg, and finish with a squeeze of lemon and a dash of pepper. Optional flavor tweaks include adding garlic, ginger, or chili flakes if you like.
Meal.nutrition_note: Well-balanced morning option with fiber from whole-grain bread, heart-healthy fats from avocado, and protein from egg. Lemon adds vitamin C; this combo provides steady energy and satiety. Note: garlic/ginger are not included in the listed ingredients.

Meal.name: Greek Yogurt Bowl — Maple-Kissed Combo
Meal.short_description: A protein-rich yogurt bowl with berries, granola, and chia seeds for a balanced mix of protein, fiber, and healthy fats.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serving idea: 1 cup Greek yogurt, 1/2 cup berries, 1/4 cup granola, 1 tablespoon chia seeds. For added sweetness, drizzle maple lightly; for a lower-sugar version, omit maple and use unsweetened yogurt with a low-sugar granola.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein-forward base from Greek yogurt; fiber from berries and chia; calcium and probiotics from yogurt; granola adds crunch and additional carbs and fats. Optional maple drizzle increases sugar; to keep sugar in check, choose unsweetened yogurt and a low-sugar granola.

Meal.name: Bean Burrito Bowl — Berry Boost Style
Meal.short_description: Bean Burrito Bowl with black beans, rice, corn, lettuce, salsa, and cheddar. Protein- and fiber-rich with calcium from cheese; Berry Boost-style variation suggested via addition of fresh berries for antioxidant boost.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Enjoy as-is, then add a Berry Boost by topping with fresh berries or serving a berry salsa alongside. For extra healthy fats, include sliced avocado and a squeeze of lime for brightness.
Meal.nutrition_note: Highlights: good plant-based protein from black beans; fiber from beans, rice, and corn; calcium from cheddar; fat content depends on cheese amount; sodium varies with salsa and cheese. Berry Boost style: add fresh berries (e.g., strawberries, blueberries) on top or mix into salsa to boost antioxidants with minimal extra calories. For a lighter option, consider part-skim cheddar or reducing cheese and adding avocado for healthy fats.

Meal.name: Tuna Pasta Salad — Smoky Wrap
Meal.short_description: Protein-rich tuna pasta salad with cucumber and cherry tomatoes tossed in olive oil; versatile for wraps or quick bowls.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Serve as a smoky wrap by spooning the tuna pasta salad into a whole-grain tortilla with a handful of greens, or enjoy as a ready-to-eat pasta salad in a bowl.
Meal.nutrition_note: Protein from tuna, complex carbohydrates from pasta, and healthy fats from olive oil provide a balanced base. Cucumber and cherry tomatoes add fiber and micronutrients. Canned tuna can be high in sodium, so consider tuna in water and drain well or control portion size to manage sodium and calories.

Meal.name: Turkey Sandwich — Garden Veg Combo
Meal.short_description: A lean turkey sandwich on whole-grain bread with lettuce, tomato, and mustard—protein-rich, fiber-forward, veggie-packed.
Meal.meal_suggestion: Great grab-and-go lunch; pair with a fruit (apple/banana) and a glass of water for a balanced meal.
